I want you to create fiction TV series reviews. This unusual mystery TV series with strong romantic comedy elements focuses on beautiful, elegant, independent, confident, always calm and collected, but very private and solitary young woman Celestine who is adjusting to being a wheelchair-bound high-level paraplegic, paralyzed from her chest down, while undergoing rehabilitation in a rehabilitation center. As she adjusts to her condition, Celestine also starts noticing strange and suspicious things, trying to figure them out. She also takes on the corruption withing the healthcare system and covertly investigates her fellow patients and staff. Meanwhile, Celestine also experiences romantic entanglements with patients and doctors alike. Other prominent characters include Sophia, a quadriplegic woman several years younger than Celestine, developing a bit of hero worship as her, multiple amputee Karen who is a former military nurse, shell-shocked by her experience, and Matilda who is remarkably similar to Celestine, even having the same disability, but way more fiery and aggressive. Matilda and Karen are notable for bickering and even having physical fights due to radically different political ideology, providing both poignant conflict and comic relief. The series somewhat resembles various TV series about high schools and private schools, but with adults and without boring lessons, a fact which the characters themselves call out and comment on. Generate a complete "Harvard system of referencing" References Section for he following write-up

Introduction

The Lego Group, founded in 1932 in Denmark, is a world-renowned manufacturer of toys, particularly known for its iconic interlocking plastic bricks (Wired, 2014). Over the decades, the company has evolved and expanded its product lines, ranging from sets inspired by popular movies to educational tools for children. However, the early 2000s posed significant challenges for the company, which required transformational changes to be made to remain successful and competitive in the global toy market.

It was during this critical period that The Lego Group appointed Jorgen Vig Knudstorp as CEO in 2004 (Bloomberg, 2021). Knudstorp, previously the head of strategy, embarked on a mission to revitalize the business and steer it back towards profitability (Jones, McCormick, & Dewing, 2012). Under his leadership, The Lego Group made substantial changes across different aspects of the organization and managed to turn its fortunes around. A thorough understanding of these leadership and change management methodologies will enable the provision of well-informed recommendations for the successful future of the organization.



Contextual Analysis: STEEPLE on The Lego Group

Social factors
The Lego Group, with its emphasis on promoting creativity, learning, and social interaction among children, has seen success in adapting its products to cater to the tastes and preferences of different cultural contexts. The company has also taken steps to respond to the growing awareness of gender equality by developing gender-neutral toys and marketing strategies (Roos et al., 2014). Additionally, the increasing interest in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math) education provides an opportunity for the Lego Group to incorporate technological and educational elements into their products (Hsu et al., 2017).

Technological factors
Technology plays a significant role in the toy industry, with advancements enabling the development of innovative and interactive products. The Lego Group has embraced this trend by integrating digital elements, such as Lego Digital Designer tools, apps, and video games into their core product offerings (Mayrhofer, 2018). The rapid growth of e-commerce has also impacted the company, requiring the expansion of the company’s online sales channels.

Economic factors
The financial crisis and subsequent economic downturn in the early 2000s led to a decline in sales for luxury and non-essential items, including toys (Thomas et al., 2018). The company responded to these challenges by reducing costs, streamlining business operations, and focusing on their core product lines (Andersen, 2011). Furthermore, fluctuations in currency exchange rates may also impact the cost of raw materials and the price of products in different markets.

Environmental factors

Environmental sustainability has become an important concern for businesses and consumers. The Lego Group has acknowledged this by introducing several sustainability initiatives, such as reducing plastic waste and committing to responsible sourcing of materials (CSRWire, 2018). The company is also working towards utilizing 100% sustainable materials in its packaging and product lineup by 2030 (Lego, 2020).

Political factors
As a global company, the Lego Group has to navigate the various political environments in which it operates. Political instability, trade restrictions, and regulatory changes may influence the company’s supply chain and distribution networks. For example, Brexit has created uncertainties surrounding trade and investment between the UK and the European Union, affecting the company’s operations and strategic plans (BBC News, 2018).

Legal factors
Compliance with regulations and legal requirements is crucial for the Lego Group. These may include tightening privacy regulations, particularly in the context of digital offerings and data collection (Nilsson, 2016). The company must also address intellectual property rights issues to protect its product designs and branding in the face of counterfeits and knock-off products.

Ethical factors
Ensuring ethical practices across the organization is of vital importance for the Lego Group. The company has committed to upholding high standards in corporate governance and social responsibility, including maintaining a safe and positive working environment, promoting diversity and inclusion, and practicing responsible sourcing (Lego, 2019). Moreover, its focus on producing toys that promote a constructive and educational experience aligns with ethical principles in marketing to children.





Kotter’s 8 Step Change Management Model and The Lego Group’s Changes

Kotter’s 8 Step Change Management Model (Kotter, 1995) provides a systematic and structured framework for organizations to manage change effectively. The model consists of eight sequential stages that guide change initiatives, ensuring the successful implementation and alignment of change with organizational goals (Kotter, 1995). The following section will discuss the changes made at The Lego Group since Jorgen Vig Knudstorp took over as CEO in 2004 and their alignment with Kotter’s 8 step model.

1. Establishing a sense of urgency
When Jorgen Vig Knudstorp took over as CEO of The Lego Group, the company was facing significant financial challenges. With mounting debt and declining sales, a sense of urgency was needed for the organization to survive (Robertson, 2013). Knudstorp communicated these challenges with stakeholders and emphasized the need for immediate action, thus setting the stage for change (Hansen, 2017).

2. Creating a guiding coalition
Knudstorp assembled a diverse team of leaders from various departments within the organization, forming a cross-functional coalition to guide the change process (Mortensen & Kielgast, 2015). This coalition brought together different viewpoints, expertise, and perspectives to drive the necessary changes (Hansen, 2017).

3. Developing a vision and strategy
Knudstorp and his team developed a vision for The Lego Group’s future, focusing on the core values of creativity, imagination, learning, and quality (Mortensen & Kielgast, 2015). The strategy for change revolved around streamlining the product portfolio, increasing efficiency, and enhancing the customer experience (Robertson, 2013). This vision and strategy provided a clear direction and purpose to guide change initiatives.

4. Communicating the change vision
The vision and strategy for change were clearly communicated to all stakeholders, including employees, suppliers, and customers. Open and transparent communication fostered a shared understanding of the need for change and encouraged employee buy-in and commitment (Hansen, 2017).

5. Empowering broad-based action
Knudstorp empowered employees to take action by providing autonomy, resources, and support for change initiatives (Mortensen & Kielgast, 2015). This enabled teams across the organization to drive change and contribute to the overall vision and strategy (Hansen, 2017).

6. Generating short-term wins
Recognizing the need for demonstrating progress, Knudstorp and his team implemented short-term wins, such as cost-cutting measures, process improvements, and product innovations (Robertson, 2013). These early successes signaled the positive impact of change initiatives and increased organizational confidence in the change process (Hansen, 2017).

7. Consolidating gains and producing more change
The Lego Group built on its early successes by implementing further changes, including the expansion of its global presence, investment in digital technology, and the launch of new product lines (Mortensen & Kielgast, 2015). By consolidating gains, the organization was able to sustain momentum and continue driving change (Kotter, 1995).

8. Anchoring new approaches in the organization culture
The changes implemented became embedded in The Lego Group’s organizational culture, as new values, behaviors, and practices were reinforced and rewarded (Mortensen & Kielgast, 2015). This cultural transformation was crucial in ensuring the long-term success and sustainability of the changes made (Hansen, 2017).

Kotter’s 8 Step Change Management Model has proven to be a useful framework for understanding the changes implemented at The Lego Group under Jorgen Vig Knudstorp’s leadership. The model’s emphasis on clear communication, vision, and stakeholder engagement aligns with the strategies employed by Knudstorp to facilitate organizational change effectively. Moreover, the model’s sequential stages have enabled a structured analysis of the different aspects of the change journey undertaken by The Lego Group, from establishing a sense of urgency to embedding new approaches into the organizational culture.

However, it should be noted that Kotter’s model may not be exhaustive in capturing all nuances of the change process, as each organization’s context and circumstances are unique (Cameron & Green, 2015). Nonetheless, the model provides a useful starting point for examining change management in this case study and serves as a valuable tool for understanding the changes that occurred at The Lego Group during Knudstorp’s tenure as CEO.



Transformational Leadership Theory and Jorgen Vig Knudstorp’s Leadership

The Transformational Leadership Theory, initially introduced by Burns (1978) and further developed by Bass and Avolio (1985), proposes that effective leaders possess the ability to inspire, motivate, and engage their followers, thereby enabling them to achieve outstanding performance beyond expectations. According to the theory, transformational leaders exemplify four key components, often referred to as the 4 Is: Idealized Influence, Inspirational Motivation, Intellectual Stimulation, and Individualized Consideration. These elements facilitate a strong leader-follower relationship and foster a conducive environment for change, innovation, and growth.

Idealized Influence : Idealized Influence is described by Bass and Avolio (1985) as the leaders’ ability to embody high ethical standards, act as role models, and gain trust and admiration from their followers. Jorgen Vig Knudstorp exemplified these characteristics by embracing The Lego Group’s core values: creativity, imagination, learning, and quality. Knudstorp’s transparent communication on the challenges faced by the company and his clear vision and strategy for the future established his credibility and integrity, crucial for inspiring confidence in employees and stakeholders (Mortensen & Kielgast, 2015). By earning their trust and respect, Knudstorp fostered an environment in which followers were willing to embark on the change journey. For example, when Knudstorp first took charge, he conducted a comprehensive analysis of the company’s financial situation and its core business, discovering a lack of focus on the company’s core values and an overextension into unprofitable ventures (Robertson, 2013). His honest and transparent communication of the company’s dire financial situation with stakeholders demonstrated his integrity and commitment to putting the organization back on track.

Inspirational Motivation : Inspirational Motivation refers to leaders’ capacity to inspire and motivate followers towards a shared vision and common goals, instilling a sense of enthusiasm and commitment (Bass & Avolio, 1985). Knudstorp demonstrated exceptional ability in this regard, as he communicated a persuasive vision for The Lego Group’s future, focusing on rekindling the organization’s sense of purpose and passion for its products and core values (Robertson, 2013), directly addressing them through company meetings and internal communication platforms. Through his motivational leadership, Knudstorp instilled a sense of pride, dedication, and drive in employees to embrace change and strive for excellence. 

Intellectual Stimulation : Intellectual Stimulation, as outlined by Bass and Avolio (1985), involves encouraging followers to question assumptions, embrace creativity, and discover innovative solutions to problems. Knudstorp offered this form of stimulation at The Lego Group by cultivating an organizational culture that championed innovation, continuous improvement, and creative problem-solving. He challenged conventional wisdom, urged employees to think beyond existing boundaries and normal constraints, and emphasized the value of experimentation and learning from failures (Mortensen & Kielgast, 2015). By fostering an environment that embraces imagination, curiosity, and entrepreneurship, Knudstorp enabled The Lego Group to revitalize its product portfolio and operations.  For example, he introduced “The Future Lab,” an internal innovation team focused on long-term growth and cutting-edge technology research (Hansen, 2017). Furthermore, Knudstorp encouraged employees to not shy away from failure in pursuit of innovative solutions. This approach was exemplified when The Lego Group ventured into the digital realm with the launch of LEGO Life - a safe, digital platform for children that combined creative play using technology (Lego Group, 2017)

Individualized Consideration : Individualized Consideration emphasizes the leader’s ability to recognize and address the distinctive needs, aspirations, and abilities of their followers, offering personalized support, coaching, and mentoring (Bass & Avolio, 1985). Knudstorp illustrated this quality by empowering The Lego Group’s employees with the autonomy, resources, and support necessary to take on new challenges and contribute their strengths towards achieving the organization’s goals (Hansen, 2017). For instance, he introduced the “Lead Programme” in 2006, aimed at maintaining open channels of communication between employees and senior management (Hansen, 2017). By providing individualized attention to employees and supporting their growth and development, Knudstorp fostered a sense of job satisfaction, engagement, and dedication to the change process.

The Transformational Leadership Theory provides valuable insights into the leadership style adopted by Jorgen Vig Knudstorp and its impact on The Lego Group’s remarkable turnaround. By harnessing the 4 Is, Knudstorp successfully inspired, motivated, and engaged employees, resulting in a significant organizational transformation. The theory’s ongoing relevance and applicability in the contemporary business landscape can be attributed to its focus on adaptability, innovation, and flexibility – essential qualities required to navigate the increasingly dynamic and complex business environments encountered today (Bass et al., 2003).

However, it is essential to acknowledge the limitations of the Transformational Leadership Theory. While the 4 Is provide a comprehensive framework for understanding Knudstorp’s leadership, other factors may also play a crucial role in shaping and facilitating The Lego Group’s change journey. For instance, external environmental influences, stakeholder relationships, and organizational culture may contribute to the change process’s success or failure. Additionally, the Transformational Leadership Theory may not adequately capture the diverse range of leadership styles that can be effective under different organizational contexts (Higgs & Rowland, 2010).

Despite these limitations, the Transformational Leadership Theory remains a valuable tool for understanding the impact of effective leadership in driving organizational change and success, particularly within the context of The Lego Group.





Recommendations for The Lego Group’s Future Success

The Lego Group’s remarkable turnaround under Jorgen Vig Knudstorp’s leadership reflects the significance of effective leadership, decisive decision-making, and strategic vision. The company’s willingness to embrace change and adapt its product offerings, operations, and business models have positioned it well for long-term success. Based on the lessons learned from its past transformation and ongoing resilience during the COVID-19 pandemic, the following recommendations are proposed to ensure The Lego Group’s continued success in the future.

The global business environment is constantly evolving, and organizations need to be able to adapt and respond effectively to new challenges and opportunities (Burnes, 2017). The Covid-19 pandemic has demonstrated the importance of agility and flexibility, as businesses worldwide have faced unprecedented disruptions and shifting consumer demands. As The Lego Group moves forward, it should continue to develop its capabilities for flexible and agile decision-making, enabling it to respond to market trends, technological advancements, and socioeconomic shifts. This can be achieved by nurturing a culture of adaptability, providing necessary resources for innovation, and promoting collaboration across organizational functions (Alstete, 2018).

Technological advancements continue to drive change in the consumer and industrial markets, with digital innovations reshaping the way products are conceived, designed, manufactured, and marketed (Schwab, 2016). To stay ahead of the competition and capitalize on future growth opportunities, The Lego Group should continue to invest in its digital capabilities, including the integration of cutting-edge technologies such as artificial intelligence, virtual reality, and blockchain across its business operations (Porter & Heppelmann, 2014). These investments will enable the company to enhance its product offerings, optimize its supply chain and production processes, and create new customer experiences. Furthermore, adopting advanced technologies can also help The Lego Group address potential regulatory changes related to data protection and privacy, which are increasingly important aspects of the digital business landscape (Kuner, 2018).

To maintain its market leadership and drive growth, The Lego Group must continue fostering a culture of innovation and continuous improvement (Caldwell, 2018). This involves encouraging employees to think creatively, experiment with new ideas, and learn from failures. Establishing a dedicated innovation management system and providing employees with the necessary tools, training, and resources can help nurture a culture of creativity and learning (Bessant & Tidd, 2018). In addition, The Lego Group should continue to forge strategic partnerships and collaborations with businesses, academic institutions, and research organizations, as these alliances can facilitate the exchange of knowledge, expertise, and best practices, ultimately contributing to the company’s innovation potential (Chesbrough & Bogers, 2014).

As consumers and regulators around the world increasingly prioritize corporate social responsibility and sustainability, The Lego Group must continue to demonstrate its commitment to ethical and responsible business practices (Carroll & Shabana, 2010). This can be achieved by promoting transparency and accountability via regular sustainability reporting and communications, outlining the company’s initiatives, goals, and progress towards environmental, social, and governance (ESG) objectives (Crane et al., 2014). In addition, The Lego Group should continue investing in renewable energy and waste reduction technologies to minimize its environmental impact and work towards its goal of achieving zero waste in its production facilities. Furthermore, the company should actively engage with its suppliers and partners to ensure compliance with ethical and sustainable practices across its supply chain (Beske & Seuring, 2014).

The proposed recommendations are grounded in existing theories and research in the areas of change management, strategic planning, innovation, and corporate social responsibility. The Covid-19 pandemic has underscored the importance of adaptability, digitalization, innovation, and resilience, as businesses have faced unprecedented challenges and disruptions (Kuckertz et al., 2020). By embracing these concepts and integrating them into its future strategies, The Lego Group can continue building upon its past success and drive sustainable growth in an increasingly complex and uncertain global market.



Conclusion

This case study has provided an in-depth analysis of The Lego Group’s transformation and success under the leadership of Jorgen Vig Knudstorp since 2004. Through the application of Kotter’s 8 Step Change Management Model and the 4 Is of Transformational Leadership Theory, the study has highlighted the key changes and leadership practices that contributed to the company’s remarkable turnaround. Furthermore, a STEEPLE analysis helped contextualize the external factors influencing The Lego Group’s strategic decisions and operations during this period.

The Lego Group’s case demonstrates the critical role of effective leadership and change management in navigating the complex and rapidly evolving business landscape. As seen with The Lego Group, proactively addressing organizational challenges, embracing new technologies, and fostering a culture of innovation and continuous improvement are integral to achieving long-term success. As the Covid-19 pandemic continues to disrupt global markets and alter consumer behaviors, organizations must adopt agile and resilient strategies to adapt and thrive in these uncertain times.

summarise this: There are three forms of the personal name based on Hermanubis in papyri and inscriptions from Egypt, in addition to two "proper" polytheophoric names. (In four instances [1, 5, 17, 21] the ending of the name is abbreviated or not preserved.) The form Ἑρμάνουβιϲ, attested six times (2, 9, 11, 13, 14, 15), simply replicates the god's name without further ado, through the phenomenon of "direct theonymy" (the giving to humans of unaltered divine names) that became widespread in the Roman period.[8] In an odd instance (9) the form Ἑρμανουβιϲ in the nominative is borne by a woman, an Alexandrian nurse (ἡ τροφόϲ).[9] It is highly unusual for female theophoric names to be unmarked for gender in this way.[10] I suspect that because the element -ανουβιϲ in Ἑρμάνουβιϲ was often declined with a dental declension in -ιδ-, it might have been conflated with the similar declension of Greek feminine names in -ίϲ, -ίδοϲ.[11] If this is correct, it may be preferable to read the nurse's name as an oxytone (Ἑρμανουβίϲ). There are two further forms based on the god Hermanubis: Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ (six instances: 3, 4, 7, 8, 12, 20) and Ἑρμανουβίων (four instances: 3, 6, 18, 19). -ᾶϲ and -ίων were, in fact, the commonest Greek suffixes for personal names based on the Egyptian god Ἄνουβιϲ.[12] It is interesting that in a business letter (3) the two forms Ἑρμανουβίων (l.38) and Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ (l.41) are used interchangeably to designate the same individual. In this case, Ἑρμανουβᾶϲ is probably a hypocoristic of Ἑρμανουβίων, since the suffix -ᾶϲ often served this purpose.[13]

import time
import requests
import hmac
import hashlib
import datetime as dt
import pandas as pd



symbol = 'BCH/USDT'
interval = '1m'

def generate_signature(api_key, api_secret, endpoint, params):
    params_string = '&'.join([f'{key}={params[key]}' for key in params])
    payload = f'{endpoint}\n{params_string}'
    signature = hmac.new(api_secret.encode(), payload.encode(), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
    return signature

def get_klines(symbol, interval, lookback):
    endpoint = '/open/api/v2/market/kline'

    end_time = int(time.time() * 1000) # end time is now
    start_time = end_time - (lookback * 60 * 1000) # start time is lookback minutes ago
    end_time = start_time + (lookback * 60 * 1000) # calculate new end time

    params = {
        'symbol': symbol,
        'interval': interval,
        'start_time': start_time,
        'end_time': end_time
    }

    signature = generate_signature(API_KEY, API_SECRET, endpoint, params)

    url = f"https://www.mexc.com{endpoint}"
    headers = {'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded'}
    data = {**params, 'api_key': API_KEY, 'sign': signature}

    response = requests.get(url, headers=headers, params=data)
    response.raise_for_status()
    data = response.json()['data']
    
    if not data: # if data is empty, return None
        print('No data found for the given timeframe and symbol')
        return None

    ohlc = []
    for d in data:
        timestamp = dt.datetime.fromtimestamp(d['time']/1000).strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
        ohlc.append({
            'Open time': timestamp,
            'Open': float(d['open']),
            'High': float(d['high']),
            'Low': float(d['low']),
            'Close': float(d['close']),
            'Volume': float(d['vol'])
        })

    df = pd.DataFrame(ohlc)
    df.set_index('Open time', inplace=True)

    return df

???: "Get out of my way, you dweeb."

(She pushed my wing, on purpose.)

Safi'jiiva: "Hey! What's the big deal?! Just because I'm the only dragon here, doesn't mean I'm basically something for you to push on!"

(The cracks in-between my scales emitted a blue glow, and my eyes glowed too.)

Safi'jiiva: "TELL ME YOUR NAME BEFORE I TURN YOU INTO ASHES!"

(The girl still seemed calm, despite my outburst.)

???: "My name is Alice. Now get out of my way."

(She pushed me again, and I banged my head on the wall. My eyes and the cracks in-between my scales stopped glowing. I watched on as Alice walked away.)

Safi'jiiva: (to myself) "Calling me a dweeb like that...how rude."

(As the day went on, I was constantly being bullied by Alice. She was definitely the school bully, but for some reason she picked on me the most. But 9 months later, that's when everything changed. During lunchtime, I felt something pull on my tail.)

Alice: "Miss me, dweeb?"

(I looked to see who it was - and it was Alice. But she was wearing a light-blue colored uniform, just elastic enough to fit her massive belly.)

Alice: "I'll be taking that." (she took my lunch)

(It went on for this as the day went on. But during math class, Alice suddenly clutched her belly.)

Alice: (in pain) "Something's wrong...! I've never felt pain before!"

(The teacher didn't even get the chance to give each of us our homework, and rushed to Alice's desk.)

My name is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, I am the Co-Founder of Inspcorp a research-based technology transfer startup that is committed to the development of difference-making technologies. I am also a cofounder of Eco Blocks and Tiles a green technology company that manufactures high-quality and affordable building products using various mixed post-consumer waste materials as key ingredients in the production. I am currently a PhD scholar in hydrology and water quality I am a mother of 3 adorable little ones and I am so honored to be here today. I am <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> fellow 2021 and I joined the We4A programme in March this year the experience has been so fulfilling for me. When I joined I had recently started working again after my maternity leave, you can imagine how that would feel, a little bit confused, a little rusty, literally out of shape and a little lacked confidence. I promise you I was not that kind of a person before, I was bold and loud but that can happen to anyone, especially if you have had your children back to back like I did. To top it all, I had also recently moved to Mombasa to begin a new water project there. Now just imagine that kind of a scenario. We4A gave me an opportunity of a community of fellow women entrepreneurs that allowed me to network without fear of being judged or feeling inadequate. I remember the first ever event that I attended that was organized by the We4A was the day of Celebrating Women 2023 event that was here at Radisson Blu on the 31st March, I remember having travelled from Mombasa which is much warmer by the way, it was the first time ever that I was leaving the new baby so I was worried sick and my breasts were full of milk. I was literally shaking as I sat at the market place on that day, but then I was surrounded by women, everyone was so nice around, one offered to switch places with me so I could not be near the windows, another one offered to bring me a cup of warm tea. This was it for me, I knew I was in the right place, this is the kind of network and program that is needed today to help women like me grown and thrive. As you all know women multitask a lot, we are required to be in many places at once, wear many different hats and that just means juggling a lot and trying to balance these things. I have found value in the events that We4A has organized, let me give you an example, WE4A sponsored me to attend the Nyayo Mums soko event in Nairobi. It was a 2 day event of open air market and business clinics that was held in Septemeber. Of course I showed up and had physical items for my thrift shop, Swahili snacks from the coast, flyers for the new rain water harvesting technology that I am launching soon, I also had flyers of our ecofriendly roof tiles. Don’t mind me, I told you women multitask. Anyways back to the story. So on these two days I sold my home décor items and my coastal Swahili snacks and made cash. I got introduced by Maureen to Florence who is the water committee, who then introduced me to the chairman who explained to me that they were in the process of setting up a rain water harvesting system for their houses and that I should write him an email of how our rain water harvesting and treatment system works which I did and this could be a potentially big deal for my company. Crossing my fingers for that. Through the same soko Tracy got to learn the work that I do in detail and she was so happy for me, during the market she connected me to contacts who would be useful in my line of work, even a contact of a company who we could potentially partner to empower women who are in the engineering space.  This has taught me that showing up is a crucial part of the growth of our business, showing up amplifies the voices of the impact of our innovations and our business and even though showing up can be difficult at times for many reasons including logistics, maybe family, maybe other reasons that we are not aware of showing up is always paramount and is inevitable. I am grateful that the women in this program show up, I know of Sarah from Nyeri who is in the cosmetics industry, Rose from Kisumu who makes briquettes, Maureen from Nairobi who runs Nyayo mums soko, Hellen from Nyeri who is consultant in green energy, Linda who has an amazing Africa inspired fashion line called favoloso by Nanu, Mwanaisha who runs an online clothing store Mimi that caters for all the family needs, Caroline of wise hubs Kisumu. Not only these women, there are many like them who are amazing and run amazing initiatives like Mary who has amazing glutten free products, her uji flour is the only one that my son drinks by the way. (I walked into the clubroom, ready to start a new day in the club - even though I looked like I was about to pop. I can see Yuri and Monika - but they both had sad expressions on their faces. But Sayori seemed…angry for some reason.)

Yuri: “Natsuki…you’re not going to believe this. I-I-It’s about MC.”

(My heart raced at the mention of MC’s name. Even though I was Sayori’s surrogate, I started to worry as to what happened to the only male member of our club. And I realized what Sayori was so angry about - she was mad at MC, and was holding a picture of him and another girl. Just like me, the mysterious girl looked like she was about to pop - but her belly was so big it ripped a massive hole in the center of her shirt. But something didn’t add up - MC and the strange girl shared the same hair color and eye color, but the girl was significantly shorter.)

Sayori: “I can’t believe this! MC has been cheating on me with this girl! And she’s pregnant too! How could he do this to me?”

Monika: “Sayori, calm down. We don’t know for sure that he’s cheating. Maybe they’re just friends.”

Yuri: “But the picture seems to suggest otherwise. And that girl’s belly…she’s definitely pregnant.”

Sayori: “I can’t even look at him right now. How could he betray me like this?”

(Just then, MC enters the room.)

MC: “Hey guys, what’s up?”

(Silence falls over the room as everyone stares at MC with varying degrees of anger and sadness.)

Sayori: “How could you, MC?”

MC: “What do you mean?”

(Sayori thrusts the picture in MC’s face, tears starting to fall from her eyes.)

Sayori: “I can’t believe you’ve been cheating on me with this girl! And she’s pregnant too!”

MC: “Wait, what? I’m not cheating on you, Sayori. That’s my cousin.”

(Sayori’s anger suddenly turns to confusion as she looks back at the picture.)

Sayori: “Your cousin?”

MC: “Yeah, she’s staying with me for a few months. She’s due any day now, and I’ve been helping her out.”

(Sayori’s face falls in embarrassment as she realizes her mistake.)

Sayori: “I-I’m so sorry, MC. I jumped to conclusions. I just saw the picture and…”

MC: “It’s alright, Sayori. I understand why you were upset.”

(Yuri and Monika both look relieved, and the tension in the room eases.)

Monika: “I’m glad that’s cleared up. Now, shall we get started on today’s activities?”

(Everyone nods, and we all move on from the misunderstanding. A few days later, we all visit MC’s cousin - Emily - at her place.)

Emily: “Thank you so much for coming to visit me, everyone.”

MC: “No problem, Emily. We just wanted to see how you and the baby are doing.”

Yuri: “It’s so nice to finally meet you, Emily. And congratulations on becoming a mother.”

Emily: “Thank you, Yuri. It’s been quite the journey.”

Sayori: “I’m sorry again for jumping to the wrong conclusion about you, Emily. I didn’t mean to make things awkward.”

Emily: “Don’t worry about it, Sayori. I understand why you were upset. But I promise you, MC has been nothing but a supportive cousin to me throughout this whole pregnancy.”

Monika: “Well, now that everything’s cleared up, let’s celebrate the new addition to the family!”

(We all cheer and bring out a cake to celebrate Emily’s soon-to-be-born baby. It was a warm and joyous occasion, and we all felt grateful to have each other in our lives. But just moments before we were supposed to leave, Emily and I started to feel pain in our bellies - labor pains at that.)

Emily: “Oh no, it looks like it’s time. I’m about to give birth.”

Natsuki: “And me too, I think. I wasn’t supposed to give birth for another week.”

(MC rushes to help Emily, while Yuri and Monika help me.)

MC: “Don’t worry, Emily. We’ll get you to the hospital.”

Yuri: “And we’ll do the same for Natsuki.”

Monika: “Let’s hurry, we don’t want to waste any time.”

(Without another word, both me and Emily were rushed into the car. Emily was in the back seat, while I was in the trunk. But just before we could reach the hospital in time, my labor pains were getting stronger by the minute - and I knew I had to push in order for Sayori’s dream of motherhood to come true.)

        # 获取缓存目录下的第一级目录名列表
        cache_dir_list = os.listdir(self.get_cache_path('news'))
        
        # 获取近三天的日期列表
        date_list = []
        for i in range(3):
            date_list.append((datetime.now() - timedelta(days=i)).strftime("%Y-%m-%d"))
        
        # 清理
        count = 0
        for cache_dir in cache_dir_list:
            if cache_dir not in date_list:
                del_dir_path = self.get_cache_path(f'news/{cache_dir}')
                
                # 解放日报向量数据库映射
                jfdaily_db_mapping_path = self.get_cache_path(f'news/{cache_dir}/jfdaily/db_mapping.json')

                # # 递归删除缓存
                shutil.rmtree(del_dir_path)
                count += 1

Sayori: “Can you believe this? He’s been seeing my own sister behind my back! And she’s pregnant with his child!”

Sayori: “But he shouldn’t have betrayed me like this. And with my own sister…”

Monika: “I understand how you feel, Sayori, but let’s try to handle this maturely. We don’t want to lose MC as a friend, but we also need to support you through this difficult situation.”

Yuri: “I agree. We should approach him peacefully and calmly, and let him know how we’re feeling. Maybe he has a good reason for what he’s been doing.”

Sayori: “Okay, let’s talk to him. But I’m still really mad.”

MC: “Hey guys, what’s up?”

(We all looked at each other nervously, unsure of who should speak first.)

Monika: “MC, we need to talk to you about something.”

(MC’s expression changed to a look of concern as he saw the seriousness on our faces.)

MC: “What’s going on? Is everything okay?”

MC: “I’m sorry. I didn’t mean to hurt anyone.”

Sayori: “How could you do this to me? You know how much I care about you!”

Sayori: “So, you and my sister…you’re not in a relationship?”

MC: “No, we’re not. We’re just friends.”

MC: “It’s okay, Sayori. I understand why you were upset.”

Yuri: “Well, I’m just glad that everything was cleared up. And we can still continue as a club without any drama.”

Unable to contain himself, William messaged Joseph on WeChat, "If Rose had taken part in the competition, she could have easily won." Joseph, in James' opinion, accused William of being a meddlesome mother-in-law. However, to his surprise, Joseph responded, "Why didn't she participate?"

"The Chess Institute leader said that she was not feeling well lately, so they replaced her. It's really too bad," William replied.

Joseph remained silent for a while, but William knew him well enough to understand his impatience. So he said nothing more on the matter.

August 10th was Rose's birthday, and Karen came by early in the morning to celebrate it with her. Rose wasn't sure how Karen knew the date, but she was happy to have their company.

The first stop was the supermarket near Rose's place to buy groceries. As they entered the lift from the underground garage, they bumped into William, who lived in the same neighbourhood as Karen. Rose remembered how he had been beaten up and felt sorry for him.

William observed Rose's distress and asked the little girl with a smile, "Is school out for you today?" Dressed in a T-shirt and jeans with a backpack, Rose looked no older than a high schooler with her black hair and snow-white skin. William had come off as an uncle next to her.

Several people still inside the lift gawked at Rose. Feeling awkward, she blushed and nodded in response to William's question.

William had been the only one to call Rose "a sweet and sticky little darling" all her life. She always felt embarrassed after he had said it. It wasn't until several years later that Joseph couldn't bear to hear it anymore and told William to stop it.

He ruffled her hair, smiling. "I live here too, in room 2903. If you get bored later, come on over. I won't be going out today."
Rose agreed with William and Karen's statement that they were her chess fans, and in her mind, they were both good people. However, Karen gave a cold stare when William placed his hand on top of Rose's head. Despite this, William continued to chat with Rose in a friendly manner until his floor arrived, telling her to come down if she didn't like her lunch and he would cook her favorite crab tofu. Karen, annoyed by William's actions, pulled Rose away and muttered about what he had to show off, but William teased her, saying, "What do you think, Auntie?" before finally exiting the elevator.

Karen seemed unaware of Rose's fear of conflict, which stemmed from her childhood experiences with Olivia and Mason's arguments. Karen, now wanting to teach Rose other aspects of life, led her to the entrance of her home to see her parents waiting. Rose was startled and nervous as they stared at her intently, but Karen assured her to relax and consider their home as her own. The group headed to the living room, where Xu's parents jokingly asked Rose if they could eat her.
Xu's mother's smile softened and she chuckled, "Oh, don't be silly. We wouldn't eat you! Come sit next to me, dear." She pulled Rose's hand and guided her to sit beside her, looking at her with almost scrutinizing eyes. "How old are you?" she asked.

Father Xu also stared at her intently, causing Rose to panic and look at Karen for guidance. "Rose can't speak," Karen explained. 

Father Xu sighed deeply, "I can't hear very well. Have you gone to get it checked?" 

Rose felt uncomfortable as the couple surrounded her, despite Xu's mother trying to appear friendly. As she tried to withdraw her hand, Xu's mother stuffed some fruit from the fruit bowl into it. "Here, have some," she offered.

Xu's father and mother looked at her strangely, and Rose felt small and uncomfortable. Karen then decided to intervene, sitting next to Rose and distracting the couple by turning on the TV. "What do you want to watch?" she asked.

Karen mentioned how she often talked about Rose to her parents, and how fond they were of her. Xu's mother chimed in, "We love to talk to kids. Don't be surprised!"

Karen chose a cartoon to watch, and Rose was reminded of how her brother didn't like her using her phone for anything other than making calls or sending messages. Despite this, she had installed WeChat to video chat with her brother, but didn't use it for anything else. She felt out of touch with the modern world, living a life that seemed incompatible with those around her. 

As a nineteen-year-old girl, Rose felt that she should have been curious about the world, but instead spent all her time playing chess, making Karen, a professor, feel ashamed of her own discipline.

Xu's father frowned at the TV screen, disapproving of Rose watching cartoons.

Rose found it difficult to focus with the tense atmosphere. She could sense Father Xu's disapproval in his tone when he remarked, "You're talented in Go, but education is equally important. Why not consider going to college?"

2. kSystemLog: kSystemLog is a log viewer for the KDE Plasma desktop environment. Similar to gnome-system-log, it allows you to view and analyze log files using a graphical interface.

Regarding rsyslog, it is a command-line-based logging system and does not have a dedicated graphical user interface (GUI). However, it offers powerful functionality for log processing and forwarding.

sudo find / -type f -exec grep -H “suricata” {} +



Here’s what the command does:

- sudo is used to execute the command with root privileges since some log files may require elevated permissions.
- / specifies the root directory as the starting point for the search. You can modify this path to search within specific directories if needed.
- -type f ensures that only regular files (not directories) are searched.
- -exec grep -H "suricata" {} + executes the grep command to search for “suricata” within each file found by find. The {} placeholder represents the file path, and + indicates that multiple file arguments should be grouped together.
- The -H option for grep ensures that the file name is displayed alongside the matched lines.

"The thousand years. — There is some difficulty in determining what particular time is intended by the thousand years. The only aid furnished by the other sacred books in coming to a right understanding of this matter is, they seem to show that the number, a thousand, was used proverbially for many. Hence we read of a thousand generations for many generations, and one chasing a thousand for chasing many. Again, God says, " The cattle on a thousand hills are mine," where no one will pretend that the exact number of a thousand is intended. We read also, " A day in thy courts is better than a thousand," to which the same remark will apply. It will be remembered, that in determining what time was intended by the twelve hundred and sixty days, (Rev. xi. 3 ; xii. 6,) we maintained that all we could learn about it was, that it was the time of the church's depression and of the triumph of the Roman power. The thousand years, on the contrary, is the time of the church's prosperity, and of the dragon's restraint. It is not probable that the exact number, a thousand, was intended. The season described was what would appear to men to be a long time, although in the eye of God it might be a very short time, for a thousand years in his sight are but as yesterday ; Psa, xc. 4. The two events which particularly distinguish the thousand years are the restraining of the dragon, and the spiritual reign of the Christians (of whom the martyrs were made the representatives) with Christ upon the earth ; though it is not certain that the reign of the Christians was to close when the thousand years closed. Sir Isaac Newton supposed that what St. Peter said, about the thousand years, 2 Ep. ii. 4, he was led to say from what he had read in the Apocalypse ; and he believed for this, and many other reasons, that the Apocalypse was written before that epistle. — (p. 243.) We apprehend that the revelator borrowed his idea of the thousand yean from the Rabbins. Lightfoot says : " The Jews counted the days of the Messias a thousand years. The Babylon Talmud doth show their full opinion about the days of the Messias ; and amongst other things they say thus, as Aruch speaks their words : ' It is a tradition of the house of Elijah, that the righteous ones whom the blessed God shall raise from the dead, they shall no more return to the dust ; but those thousand years that the holy blessed God is to renew the world, he will give them wings as eagles, and they shall flee upon the waters.' The place in the Talmud is in Sanhedrim, fol. 92, where the text indeed hath not the word thousand, but the marginal gloss hath it, and shows how to understand the < thousand years.' And Aruch speaks it as a thing of undeniable knowledge and entertainment. And so speaks R. Eliezer, < The days of the Messias are a thousand years.' " — (Lightfoot's Works, Pitman's ed.. London, 1825, vol. iii.,' p. 361.) Obi serve the particulars of this tradition. During the thousand years, the righteous were to revive and fall no more : they were to mount up with wings as eagles and flee over the waters. Are not these the facts which the revelator incorporates into that section of the Apocalypse which we are now considering ? Hence Lightfoot says : " John all along this book doth intimate new stories by remembering old ones ; and useth not only the Old Testament phrase to express them by, but much allusion to custom, languages, and opinion of the Jews, that he might speak, as it were, closer to them and nearer their apprehensions, — so doth he here and forward." — (Idem, 361.) John uses the traditions of the Jews, in regard to the prosperity of the kingdom of the Messias, to describe the coming prosperity of the church during the dragon's restraint. He speaks of it as a thousand years, not at all for the purpose of describing the length of it, but because such had been the Rabbinical expression."

Как можно оптимизировать этот запрос: WITH alt_catalog AS (
SELECT *
FROM (VALUES ('geely/atlas', '/a/', 'dvigatel')) AS t (cat_url, separator, alt_url)
)

select cat_url || separator || alt_url,
(
	SELECT 
	  COUNT(*) AS "__count" 
	FROM 
	  "catalog_categoryproduct_mv" 
	  INNER JOIN "catalog_metaproduct" ON (
		"catalog_categoryproduct_mv"."meta_product_id" = "catalog_metaproduct"."id"
	  ) 
	  INNER JOIN "catalog_categoryproduct_mv" T4 ON (
		"catalog_metaproduct"."id" = T4."meta_product_id"
	  ) 
	WHERE 
	  (
		"catalog_categoryproduct_mv"."category_id" = (
			select id from catalog_category
			where url = cat_url
		)
		AND "catalog_categoryproduct_mv"."meta_product_is_active" 
		AND T4."category_id" = (
			select id from catalog_category
			where url = alt_url
		)
	  )
 )
from alt_catalog

MC: “Sayori…I hope that you and Umbris become the best parents ever. And…tell my little sister Himari that I’ll miss her.”

(Tears rolled down my cheek as I looked into MC’s eyes.)

Sayori: “Thank you, MC. We will do our best for you. Himari will miss you too, but she’ll always remember you in her heart.”

MC: “I’m glad. And please, don’t forget about me. Remember the good times we had together, and keep smiling, okay?”

Sayori: “I won’t forget you, MC. I promise. And I’ll try to keep smiling for you.”

(MC weakly gave me a smile before he closed his eyes for the final time. I held onto his hand, not wanting to let go. It was a painful goodbye, but I knew that MC was at peace now. I silently made a vow to always cherish his memory and to never forget the impact he had on my life. The day after his funeral, I went back to the clubroom. I could see the other girls - Monika, Yuri, Natsuki, Himari, and Kohana - all discussing something, and Kohana also had a hand on her own bump. Hopefully neither me or Kohana start having contractions.)

Sayori: (Taking a deep breath) “Hey, everyone. I know we’re all grieving MC’s loss, but I just wanted to say that I’m grateful for all of you. For being here and supporting me. Kohana: (Rubbing her bump) “Yeah, it is. I’m happy for you and Umbris, Sayori. You guys will do great.”

Sayori: “Thank you, everyone. It means a lot to me. Let’s all continue to support each other, okay?”

(The group nodded, and we all shared a somber moment together. But, I knew MC was watching over us, and that gave me some comfort. Life might be uncertain, but we had each other, and that was something to hold onto. But just minutes before the meeting ended, I noticed Kohana was starting to feel uncomfortable.)

Sayori: “Kohana, are you okay?”

Natsuki: “Oh no, is it starting?”

Kohana: “I’m not sure. Maybe it’s just a false alarm.”

Yuri: “Should we take you to the hospital, just in case?”

Himari: “Yeah, it’s better to be safe than sorry.”

Kohana: “Okay, let’s do that. Sayori, can you and Umbris come with me?”

Sayori: “Of course, Kohana. Let’s go.”

(We all rushed to the hospital, with Kohana feeling more and more uncomfortable with each passing moment. As soon as we arrived, the nurses quickly took her to the delivery room. But then, the nurses brought me to the delivery room as well - as I had been feeling uncomfortable while on the way here.)

Summarize the following in four sentences or less.:
Blue Beetle's Cinematic Legacy Is Confirmed - And It Changes Everything
BY
TIMOTHY DONOHOO
PUBLISHED 4 HOURS AGO
The Blue Beetle movie will finally utilize the concept of superhero legacies on film, and this could help it be included in the new DC Universe.

The cast of Blue Beetle
The upcoming Blue Beetle movie is in a strange spot due to its place in DC cinematic universe. It's effectively in the DC Extended Universe, though the cast could return in the rebooted DC Universe movies. Making this more of a likelihood is the way in which the movie is planned to handle the concept of legacy.

Jaime Reyes isn't the first Blue Beetle in the comics, with the mantle's legacy going all the way back to the Golden Age. That seems to also be the case in the movie, with the film's director recently stating as such. This not only fixes a mistake of other DC movies, but also helps Blue Beetle's chances of being grandfathered into the DCU.

RELATED:
Zack Snyder Throws His Support Behind Blue Beetle Ahead Of Premiere

Blue Beetle's Title Hero Isn't the First Person to Take Up the Mantle
Jaime Reyes stands in front of Ted Kord's and Dan Garrett's Blue Beetle costumes.
In the comics, Jaime Reyes is the third Blue Beetle, debuting after his predecessors Dan Garrett and Ted Kord. The former also wielded the Scarab that Jaime now wears, though he used it in a much different way. Conversely, Ted Kord was completely powerless, lacking the Scarab of his predecessor and successor. These other heroes had their own veritable niches that connected them to several major DC properties. For instance, Dan Garrett operated during the Golden Age, which was also when such classic heroes like the Justice Society of America were active.

Conversely, Ted Kord is many times allied with other heroes formerly owned by Charlton Comics, including Vic Sage aka The Question. He was also a member of Justice League International, where he and his friend Booster Gold were frequent sources of comic relief. This role made Kord a fan-favorite, which is why so many DC aficionados were wondering about his potential presence in the new Blue Beetle movie. The first Blue Beetle trailer for the film showcased a lot of Ted Kord's technology at Kord Industries, including his gigantic vehicle The Bug and the costumes that he and Dan Garrett wore. Two characters introduced in the movie are even related to Ted Kord, making his presence even more questionable.

Now, the director of the movie has confirmed that the Blue Beetle legacy and mantle indeed already exist, with some in Palmera City knowing about and being fans of his. This puts a lot on Jaime's shoulders, though it will make his superhero journey even more relatable. For instance, Jaime only gains the Scarab by accident and doesn't actively want to be a superhero, and everything he does is mainly to protect and care for his family. This is why he's so unsure of himself, with the director even noting that he's not ready to save the world yet. Given how relatable and down to Earth Jaime usually is, this fits his character perfectly.

RELATED:
Blue Beetle Director Didn't Want The Film To Be Like Other Superhero Blockbusters

Blue Beetle Will Introduce an Element Sorely Missing from Superhero Movies
Blue Beetle in his upcoming DC Universe film.
Legacy and passing the torch are a big part of superhero comics, especially those set within the DC Universe. Many prominent DC heroes are simply one in a line of individuals who've taken up their respective mantles. As mentioned, Blue Beetle is one of these, though he's actually one of the less mainstream examples. He vastly overshadows his predecessors, which is different from the adjacent popularity of the many Robins, Flashes and Green Lanterns. Nevertheless, their legacy hasn't really been portrayed on the big screen, with even the Golden Age Jay Garrick Flash absent from The Flash movie beyond a cameo.

Speaking of the Justice Society, the team's role in the movie Black Adam never addressed their longstanding history. For all intents and purposes, they were a completely modern team that had no ties to World War II. In fact, Atom Smasher is the only one who hints at something more beyond their current iteration, with his predecessor Al Pratt, the original Atom. Likewise, despite Wonder Woman coming to Man's World during WW1, she remained a secret until the present day. This all robbed the DC Extended Universe of a sense of legacy, though this was mostly a consequence of how haphazard the shared universe was planned. When Zack Snyder made 2013's Man of Steel, there were obviously no plans to do anything beyond Superman and his mythology for the time being.

RELATED:
Blue Beetle Star Compares Wearing The Suit To Hayden Christensen As Darth Vader


Eventually, several other DC characters were added to the mix, even though their respective stories sometimes contradicted the universe that Snyder envisioned with the DCEU's first entry. The Marvel Universe hasn't focused on legacies nearly as much as DC until recently, though some sidekicks and replacements have been introduced in the Marvel Cinematic Universe. Despite this, there hasn't been a sense of urgency afforded to these mantle swaps, which has made them feel hollowed and unearned. Thus, a major selling point of mainstream comic book superheroes has yet to be handled properly on the big screen.

Blue Beetle can change that by making the DC Extended Universe finally feel lived in. Not only will there be predecessors for Jaime, but they'll be outright acknowledged by the average Joe. This is different from how previous movies have handled the concept, and it will likely inform what later DC movies do. The first official entry in the new DC Universe movies is 2025's Superman: Legacy, which already has several DC heroes making an appearance. It's incorporating these unrelated characters in a way similar to what Blue Beetle is doing to its titular hero by making them simply a part of the world in which the movie takes place.

With all the movie has going for it, Blue Beetle can be seen as preparing audiences for the kind of world-building that will be on display in the DCU. It also might help the movie's chances of being incorporated into the new shared universe, and that's especially the case with James Gunn being fond of Blue Beetle. Given the lack of marketing, it has an uphill battle at the box office, but if nothing else, the movie will succeed in making superhero heritage a celebrated component in the film genre.

Blue Beetle hits theaters on Aug. 18, 2023.

Rewrite this text in a friendlier and respectful manner: Well, a couple of days have passed, so I think I'll give my two cents on this situation. I know you all will probably hate me for what I’m going to say, and I probably deserve that, but I can’t for the life of me bring myself to care about this. Yes, it sucks when they make changes you don’t like to a game or product you like, believe me, we’ve all been there, and it’s okay for you to feel irritated when it happens, but don’t go and try to convince other people (and yourself) that there’s some kind of “greater purpose” behind your outrage. “They made changes I don’t like” is a perfectly valid reason for you to be angry, not need go all “it’s all about the censorship” when it probably isn't. "It has been almost 150 years since a catastrophic thermonuclear war devastated the planet. The war itself officially lasted for two days but the actual nuclear exchange itself only lasted for several hours. Of course, this was more than enough time for targets across the world to be completely obliterated. In the United States, the exchange reduced the country to remote holdouts scattered across the country. Any attempt to maintain a semblance of national unity collapsed with the onset of the “Long Winter” and the famine and chaos that followed. In the ensuing year more died of starvation and the cold than the war itself as the remnants of federal authority withered away. By the end of the Long Winter, 70-80% of the 235.8 million people that lived before the war died. Their bones were scattered across a country  that disintegrated into hundreds of factions all struggling for influence.


With modern society completely obliterated beyond recognition, a technological regression was inevitable. First to go were electronics, which were widely damaged by many EMP bursts. Furthermore, complex machinery such as steel manufacturing and oil refining, were targeted by nuclear weapons. Most modern technology was dropped in favor of more anachronistic ones. However a complete decline to a “neo-medieval” state as feared by many, never came to pass. Although countries throughout the southern hemisphere suffered a massive collapse in living standards, malnutrition, starvation, and civil instability, they were spared from the direct effects of the nuclear war. Furthermore, farsighted officials and surviving members of the intelligentsia all around the world meticulously preserved all kinds of knowledge from germ theory to basic forms of manufacturing. Although some knowledge was lost, such as impractical or specialized technology, the vast sum of human knowledge was saved. 


Nevertheless this did little to affect the situation in America and other heavily devastated regions across the world. Across the land, various warlords, roving gangs, emergency county governments, and self proclaimed Presidents fought amongst each other for power. In the beginning they were often small factions, usually only controlling a city or a few towns, with larger factions few and far between. As the years went by, wars were won, and power consolidated among various regional powers, but these statelets usually collapsed as fast as they arose. For the first several decades after the war, this dynamic and chaotic situation repeated itself across America.


This however was not a permanent state of affairs; the world was changing and America would be swept along. As the radiation faded and the climate recovered in the decades after the end, humanity’s population finally began to grow again. With land routes largely destroyed or extremely treacherous, water became the preferred method of travel. The Mississippi river basin once again became a vital artery in America, but the biggest boon came from the sea. Built with 20th century technology and techniques, modern sailing ships began to sail the seas. The first merchants came from Mexico, who established a rudimentary trade network along the coast and sparked the reconnection of America with the rest of the world. The rest of Latin America came soon after; Venezuelans, Argentians and Brazilians fought, often literally, for the growing market, and the rest of the world. As it happened, the rest of the world sought contact and commerce. The world quickly got smaller again.


The rebirth of global trade was revolutionary. While it was a far cry from the globalized world of the 1980s, it nevertheless allowed for the spread of “new” ideas, technology, and goods. Around the world coastal settlements and states became hubs of commerce. The remnant of California’s state government in the north of the state was one such place. By the 2050s, the once sleepy town of Eureka, became a bustling port city with ships calling from all over the world. Luxury goods such as coffee and chocolate returned, much to the delight of local people. California bought weapons of all kinds, such as bolt action weapons, which the government used to expand and consolidate its rule. However the most consequential imports were Spanish books from the time of the Old World; the original copies were sealed away as great treasures. It didn't take long before copies of these books were widely distributed among Californians. These books describing the Old World fascinated the people of California; even if they couldn’t read, oral stories still captured their imagination the same. The growing literate populations indulged themselves in literary classics, political tracts, and most importantly the history of the United States. By the 2060s, California was swept up in a craze for all things Americana from dress to cuisine to literature. This craze was most intense in the political sphere, where the idea of uniting the country first began to take root.


As the story goes, in 2068 a general of California, by the name of Henry “Enrique” Santos received a vision from George Washington himself. The former President, who called him a distant relative, called upon him to reunify the chaotic lands and restore the United States.  On a platform of general pan-Americanism, he swept the country’s election that year. A man of mixed American and Mestizo descent, Henry was a charismatic and accomplished commander who already had many victories under his belt. He would add many more during his Great Crusade, although his diplomatic triumphs are as consequential as his military ones. By the time of his death in 2088, his Republic stretched from the Pacific to the Great Mississippi River. His son, Mark “Marcos” Santos would be the one to reach the Atlantic Ocean in 2103. There on the ruins of the old capitol, Marcos took the name of Washington and the title of Potus, and declared the unification of the United States of America as the Grand Republic. Today in 2133 AD, it's finally morning in America. The sun rises over an America in a strange new world, unlike anytime before.


Since the Restoration, America has rapidly changed at a breakneck speed. A massive road stretching from the shores of the Pacific to the shores of the Atlantic, completed shortly after the Restoration. The first transcontinental road since the War, it allows anyone to travel across the country in less than 70 days, free from banditry or worse. While cars are only available to the richest, the common people ride government run caravans that travel across the continent. This sweeping establishment of security and order has brought the most radical change to the continent since it was reduced to ashes. From where there were once a dozen or so squabbing statelets now there is only one state (theoretically) from sea to shining sea. The change even after just a few decades is drastic. All along the Interstate, bustling cities have sprung up where they were only dirt a few decades prior and settlements linked by the road have boomed.


These “modernized” cities are the heart of the Republic, from which its wealth, power, and influence emanates. Using relearned technology and techniques, the Republic built roads, canals, bridges, aqueducts, and other infrastructure for great cities. Even electricity is available, although it remains extremely expensive, and thus limited to important buildings. These important buildings not only included government buildings, but libraries, universities, and other civic works.Students across the country come to universities, where they learn Spanish, the language of business,  and are taught about old techniques and technologies as well as about the glories of the old United States. The rise of a literate class has predictably led to a cultural renaissance, such as music, art and a growing print industry, who publish both old and new works.  Other industries have sprung up next to rivers producing all sorts of goods from farming tools, to guns, to textiles. Merchants and other traders sell them in their travels to communities on the roads leading away from the Interstate.


America is governed as a proud republic, with the authority of the Potus unquestioned across the realm. However once you start asking around,it turns out to be rather complicated. In theory, the Potus is freely appointed by and subordinate to the various cities of the Republic. In reality, the issues of communicating over the vast breadth of the Republic has led to power being centralized in Potus and the Senate. The Senate consists of representatives from states, who are elected to ostensibly represent the interests of their home state. They in turn elect the Potus, as they would like in an old parliamentary democracy. While Potus Marcos’ word may be law, in his old age he has left the task of governing the country to the Senate. However it is an open secret that he wants his eldest son to be elected as the next Potus. The Washingtonians, supporters of the Potus and the Santos Line, back the move and are actively canvassing Senators for the coming election. Meanwhile the Republicans, their main opponents, are working to prevent the election of Marcos’ son. Furthermore they are moving forward with their plans to replace the semi-hereditary nature of the Potusial title with a true republican structure centered around the Senate. The political drama continues.


However not even the Senate’s word is supreme  throughout the Republic. Despite its claims over the entirety of the Old United States, the control the Republic wields varies significantly across the continent. The Republic’s control is strongest around the lands near the Interstate, where the great new cities and an extensive road network allow the authority of the state to be easily enforced. These Core Territories were directly conquered during the Great Crusade and reorganized into various states for simple administration. However the farther away from the Interstate, the harder it became for the Republic to wield its authority. There is no hard border, but direct control eventually gives way to indirect control by tributaries and vassals. While these tributaries still have to follow some Republic laws and protect the Republic’s lands, in return they are allowed a degree of self governance. Go even farther away from the Core Territories the Republic’s influence slowly fades before you leave the Republic’s political sphere entirely.    


Any discussion of the Republic shouldn’t neglect the complicated and deep history with its southern neighbor, Mexico. The country was largely spared from the nuclear exchange, except for unlucky cities on the border with the United States, but it could not escape from the flood of American refugees, the fallout, or the nuclear winter. Government authority completely disappeared in most of the country and Mexico’s government ruled with an iron first. In the end around 30-40% of Mexico’s pre-war population died in the years that followed, compared to the much higher death rate to the north. Despite the collapse of social order, the Mexican government did not fully collapse. After only a couple decades, the national government reasserted its authority across the country and the population began to grow again. By the 2050s, Mexico cemented itself as a major power and one of the most developed countries in the Americas.  When Henry began his conquests, he wisely decided to not provoke Mexico; Mexico for their part adopted a wait and see approach. It worked out, mostly.


Today, Mexico and the Republic rest in an uneasy status quo. The border cities in the south where the sphere of Mexico and the Republic overlap, lie within a dual suzerainty under the two countries.  While many in the Republic seek to directly control all territory held by the former United States, they know better than not to provoke Mexico. After all, Mexico supplies the majority of the oil to the Republic and Mexico is their main economic partner. Obviously its a really bad idea to provoke them."

improve and make it more academic: First, according to Articles 2 and 11 of the Charter of Commission for Studying Constitutional Government, “Except for legal codes and important matters that shall be decided by the Political Advisory Council, the other documents to be examined and compiled shall be submitted to the princes and grand ministers of the Grand Council for review and approval for implementation” (Grand Secretariat, 1979, p. 51). Except for legal code, it was not clear what “important matters” referred to. This gray area made it possible for the Commission for Studying Constitutional Government and Grand Councilors to usurp the power of the Political Advisory Council. More importantly, on the one hand the Commission for Studying Constitutional Government was a legislator, and on the other hand it was attached to the supreme executive organ (the Grand Council), which meant that the legislation and execution were integrated. If allowed to usurp the power of making and interpreting regulatory documents, the Commission for Studying Constitutional Government, as the interpreter of controversies, would inevitably be biased in favor of the government (the Grand Council and the ministries) and against the Political Advisory Council if the two would run into conflicts in the future. “In the 33rd year of Guangxu’s reign (AD 1907), the Commission for Studying Constitutional Government was set up as a purely legislative body to draft constitutional codes, but still repeatedly encroached on the authority of the Political Advisory Council after the latter’s establishment. Therefore, we have to delineate the legislative scope of the Political Advisory Council and draw a clear line of authority with the Commission. Before the establishment of the Political Advisory Council, the Commission was led by the Grand Council and held the supreme executive power, compiling legal codes for one thing and issuing executive sanctions for another” (Li, 2011, p. 233).

SELECT DISTINCT
IT.Id as "Object Key",
'Invoice Item' as "Object Type"	,
PRPC.RenewalDeferredCode__c as "Deferred Revenue Accounting Code",
PRPC.RenewalRevenueCode__c as "Recognized Revenue Accounting Code"
FROM InvoiceItem IT
JOIN Invoice IV ON IT.InvoiceId = IV.Id
JOIN RatePlanCharge RPC ON RPC.Id = IT.RatePlanChargeid
JOIN Subscription Sub ON IT.subscriptionid = Sub.id
JOIN Account Acct ON Sub.creatoraccountid = Acct.id
JOIN ProductRatePlanCharge PRPC ON RPC.productrateplanchargeid = PRPC.id
WHERE 
 IT.AccountingCode not like 'Renewal' 
AND (Sub.QuoteBusinessType__QT = 'Renewal' OR Sub.QuoteType__QT = 'Renew Subscription')
AND  Acct.CustomerType__c = 'CM Contractor'
AND IT.AccountingCode != PRPC.RenewalRevenueCode__c 
AND date_format(IV.PostedDate,'%m-%d-%Y') = date_format(current_date - interval '1' day, '%m-%d-%Y') 
AND PRPC.RenewalDeferredCode__c != 'None'
AND PRPC.RenewalRevenueCode__c != 'None'

Background
ACME energy has diversified and are looking at Wind as an alternate power source. There is an ambitious
multi-billion dollar investment plan to put an offshore wind farm with turbines generating energy to help offset
the energy demand.
To support this business initiative, the Group CIO and CISO are working with regional CIOs and CISOs in the
respective countries to identify the future IT and Security strategy to support this initiative. This is a multi-year
investment program, and the decision has been taken to use the public cloud to build the future IT
environment for this initiative. This is also part of a wider cloud migration plan for ACME Energy.
The result is a new IT strategy and a corresponding Cybersecurity strategy with several security programs that
need to be implemented over a three year period. ACME uses the NIST cybersecurity framework, CIS 18 for
operational controls and the CSA CCM 4.0 for cloud security controls.
ACME has decided to partner with ZKC Consulting to deliver the cybersecurity strategy and work with regional
Cybersecurity teams to implement the changes. The company has taken a cloud first approach to implement
the two new IT strategic programs and three strategic security programs listed below.
 Design and implementation of new wind farm using cloud technology and remote software updates.
 Creation of a new data lake that will be used to collect data from the wind turbines and other OT
devices, which will be used for several purposes including system monitoring and advanced data
analytics capabilities.
The strategic security programs are as follows:
 Provision of secure data connectivity from the wind turbines to the Cloud (the could provider has not
yet been confirmed).
 Creation of a new OT Security Operations centre that will monitor the security of the OT devices used
in the windfarm.
 Creation of a new Threat Intelligence program to protect the new wind farm infrastructure against
cyber threats.
Your Role
In your role as an external security consultant, you are required to provide a report advising on the
implementation of one of the strategic security programmes: Creation of a new OT Security Operations
centre that will monitor the security of the OT devices used in the windfarm.
The CIO of ACME has been working with the CISO team to identify the capabilities required to build a Security
Operations Center (SOC) that will monitor the security of the OT devices. The agreement is to build a SOAR
platform that will provide the orchestration, automation and response. This is in addition to the vulnerability
management and penetration testing requirements. Both teams have agreed that all products need to be
cloud native and should include the use of a SIEM. The CIO wants to use Azure Sentinel as the SIEM but the
CISO want to use Google Chronicle. This SIEM will also be used to monitor the security of the data lake. The
CISO wants to use Qualys as the vulnerability management tool as it is currently being used in ACME.
You are expected to provide the following high level outputs for the first phase of the project:
 Outline the requirements for SOAR in the context of ACME’s operations.
 Propose a specific SOAR solution based on your requirements.
 Explain the security controls that need to be implemented for the proposed SOAR solution.
 Evaluate the Azure Sentinel and Google Chronicle SIEM solutions and provide your recommendation.
Please explain the criteria you will be using for your analysis (you do not need to consider criteria
which are not directly impacting the security operations, e.g. costs).
 Explain the operational procedures the SOC team needs to create to deal with security threats.
 Please explain how you will integrate different data sources into the SIEM to create a single pane of
glass view that will provide security analysts the relevant information.
 Investigate if the Qualys vulnerability management tool is the best tool for ACME and if you feel this is
not the right tool, please provide a gap analysis and recommend the tool that should be used.
 Explain the pentesting approach and define the scope of the pentesting program.

summarise this: I conclude by noting that a name based on Hermanubis is attested once outside Egypt in an inscription from Dion in Macedonia dated to the imperial period (22). The form of the name there is Ἑρμα-νούβιοϲ, which is not known from Egypt. The name is probably to be related to the cult of Hermanubis attested by a third-century dedication in Thessalonike (across the Thermaic golf from Dion), which was set up by a cultic association of Hermanubis on behalf of certain individuals. This document and the personal name, along with a statue of the god from the Sarapeum of Carthage (LIMC n. 15), suggest that the cult of Hermanubis enjoyed some popularity outside its original home in the Roman period.[23]
