write main idea of this text:
It's graduation day at the small New England college where my dad taught. It's a burst of color and excitement. When I was little, he'd carry me up on his shoulders and I'd marvel at the pageantry. I loved the wizard-like academic robes that all the professors wore. My father explained how you could tell what degree someone had and the school they'd gone to, based on the color and design of their gown and hood. It was like a diploma, a credential that you could wear. I couldn't wait to have one of my own.
My dad taught biochemistry for decades and was one of those beloved professors. Graduation, for him, was a frenzy of thankful hugs and handshakes, eager introductions to parents. Countless careers in science and medicine were launched in his Bio 101 class, and it was clear he made a difference.
I suppose I inherited a love of teaching from my dad, and I planned to be a professor, just like him. I headed to grad school to do a PhD, studying microbiology and microchemistry. But along the way, my dad's path and mine started to diverge.
During long days in lab, I got impatient. I wanted to teach. I didn’t want to wait to graduate and do a postdoc, spend years navigating a treacherous job market. So I turned to the internet, which was breaking down the barriers to entry and removing the traditional gatekeepers in many established fields. From my kitchen table, I started recording tutorial videos, teaching the basics of chemistry using simple, hand-drawn diagrams. I put the videos on YouTube ... and people actually started watching them. First, a few folks, and then more, and then more. Thank-you emails poured into my inbox. I wasn't a professor yet, I hadn't even finished my PhD, but I was helping students all over the world get through their chemistry classes.
I got the sense that something big was brewing here. But my dad would hear none of it. When I explained that I was thinking about abandoning the traditional professorship route to explore this new world of online education, he exploded in anger. "Oh, Tyler, you'd have to be an idiot to think that anyone would care about this stupid YouTube thing." I shot back with a dig. I said, "Every single day, my videos teach 10 times as many students as you'll teach in your whole career." It would have really hurt, if he'd had any conception of what I was trying to describe.
But maybe he was afraid to think about the way his world, steeped in tradition, was on the brink of change. It used to be that students had one professor, the one standing at the front of the lecture hall. But increasingly, if that professor wasn't a good fit, students could go online and seek out videos from other educators to help them learn. It was like an online marketplace where students could essentially choose their own professor. And it was free. Some of these video creators were instructors from institutions, but others could be brilliant teachers who didn't even have a college degree. Students chose the teachers that help them learn best, and the most popular teachers rose to the top.
I wanted to bring my dad into this new world. I suggested we create a video series for intro biology. His lectures, crafted and perfected over decades, took terrifying subjects like the Krebs cycle and transcription and made them crystal clear and beautiful. They could help millions of students a day. "Why would any other college need a Bio 101 professor?" I joked. But it was something I'd seriously thought about a lot. What if you identified a few incredibly talented educators like my dad and gave them essentially limitless resources, content editors, and animators and production teams, and they were able to devote all day, every day, to making incredible, beautiful educational content. It seemed like this could fundamentally change a field where many professors all around the world were teaching essentially similar courses, particularly at the introductory level. But each professor rarely had the time and resources to go all in. Incredible education content felt like something that could scale, a key concept driving so many of the new revolutions in tech.
"You could be the world's biology teacher," I said. "Oh, you have to be an idiot to think that I'd want to be some kind of YouTube star." Ugh. I was furious.
And then, shortly thereafter, unexpectedly, he died ... right before I graduated from MIT. It upended my life. But there was a silver lining that felt slightly cosmic. He left me a little bit of money that allowed me to step off the academic path and try my own thing.
I poured myself into work, churning out videos day and night. And I also started to interact more with my viewers. And I learned that they were almost all folks who weren't served well by the rigid structure of traditional academia. Countless college students told me how they did all their learning from videos like mine. They attended class only three or four times during a semester, just to take the tests. Others were trying to switch careers in middle age, and they needed to take courses piecemeal. They needed half of this degree and a quarter of this one. A single dad writes me and says how he's trying to go to nursing school, to show his young daughters he can be something. He can't understand a word his professor says, but my videos get him through a critical class. Comments like his are often followed by an ominous ... “So why am I paying the school, and not paying you?”
I wonder why these people have to go through the motions of attending a class, when they are learning all the material on their own. Why can't they get course credit in other ways? Why isn't anyone paying attention to what these people need? I can't offer diplomas from my YouTube channel, but once there is a way for students to earn course credit no matter how they learned the material, in class or on their own, the online marketplace of different teachers and different learning approaches will explode. There will be serious competition for who could teach students the best.
Meanwhile, as I madly upload videos, my views go through the roof. Job offers start to come in. Random people start recognizing me on the street, an awkward "Hey, um .... Do you make YouTube videos?" It's followed by hugs and handshakes, selfies, and even occasional tears. Around this time, my career moves from the lecture hall to the laboratory. I joined a company focused on education for pharmaceutical and life-sciences companies. The CEO is bold and eccentric, and she wants to push the envelope and teach complex lab methods entirely in virtual reality.
Outside of academia, things move fast, and the stakes are different. The goal for me used to be a final exam grade. Now, it's a patient's health, a life-saving therapy.
For the team I joined, it was a rare chance to think deeply about lab instruction. In undergrad, I rarely knew what I was doing in labs. A couple of drops of this and a couple of drops of that, and poof -- it would turn red. Test tubes would break, a frazzled TA tried to guide 30 students at the same time. But VR can be a consistent, constantly vigilant one-on-one coach. A learner can practice activities over and over, until they truly understand what they're doing and why they're doing it. And students don't need an instructor or a TA -- the software does the teaching. Put on a VR headset, and you don't need a multi-million-dollar microbiology laboratory to teach microbiology.
And it's clear that academia isn't the only player that can provide high-quality learning even in advanced technical fields. Coding boot camps have received a lot of attention giving credentials that allow folks to transition into programming roles. But with VR, you could imagine companies offering biotech credentials and teaching lab skills needed to, say, manufacture cutting-edge cell and gene therapies.
When you look at all of these forces together, it's clear that real change is going to come in higher education. When I was up there on my father's shoulders, those colored robes at graduation represented discrete credentials, earned from classroom and research time at specific schools. Maybe the metaphorical academic robe of the future is more of a patchwork cape? A discussion class taken in person at MIT, an introductory content certification passed with the help of YouTube videos. A VR laboratory course from a company outside of academia. Graduation is unlikely to be a single, defined event. And learners, instead of institutions, will have the power to decide what sort of credentials they need and when and how they master the prerequisites.
The impact of COVID is likely to only accelerate this. Even the most prestigious schools are now giving credit for courses completed online. It's going to be tough to put that genie back into the bottle. And hopefully, the specter of these changes will force colleges and universities to double down on what they can uniquely provide. Maybe that's getting students to dive into research, or providing valuable one-on-one time with professors, fostering discussions and mentorship. MIT has always been on the forefront of innovation, and there's a unique opportunity here for it to lead academia into this new future.
But look, I know how hard this change is going to be. My father, who was a brilliant educator, couldn't see it or didn't want to see it. You would have to be an idiot to think that anything was going to change. But at the same time, he valued learning over all else, like many other great teachers. He used to say the focus of education should be learning, not teaching. These new paths of teaching, of certification, they're not trivial shortcuts. They'll help students master the same fundamental skills, just more effectively, more efficiently.
You know ... I think back to the first time I tried the virtual reality lab product I'd helped to build. A team of so many brilliant, talented people had worked on it for months. I slipped on the VR headset, and there I was, a lab bench in front of me. The focus was a fundamental microbiology method. It was probably one of the first things my dad learned when he was in grad school, and it was one of the first things that I had learned. I wondered, for a moment, what he would have thought if he could have seen this. I imagined, somewhat hopefully, of course, that he'd look around in the headset, grab a petri dish, sterilize his metal tools in the Bunsen burner until they glowed a bright orange, and maybe he'd say, with one of his trademark phrases, "Whoa ... This is pretty nifty. You would have to be an idiot if you can't tell ... this is the future of education."

Please re-write this letter of support from my supervisor, so that it instead becomes an application letter (1 page). It is for an application for the Heffter Research Institute researcher travel grant. The conference I am to use the grant for is ASSC26 (association for the scientific study of consciousness). I will present some of my original research there - I will also paste below the abstract for my presentation.


Letter of support——
This letter confirms my support of James Sanders’ application for your early career investigator grant program. James has been working with the Centre for Psychedelic Research at Imperial College London, under my supervision, since early 2022. During this time James has assisted with data collection in the lab and in the field, focusing primarily on the phenomenological (especially qualitative) aspects of our research. He has also worked on analysing the collected data. 
James’ accepted talk, ‘Towards a micro-phenomenology of the intravenous DMT experience’ is the culmination of James’ contributions over this past year, featuring not only new insights into the little-understood phenomenology of DMT experience, but also novel methods in the study of non-ordinary consciousness that he has contributed greatly to developing. This research is the first ever application of the micro-phenomenological analysis method to psychedelic interview data, and the results of this analysis will be used in a forthcoming neuro-phenomenological analysis of the first ever combined fMRI/EEG imaging study of psychedelic experience (for which the preceding neurobiological results of the same study were recently published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences [PNAS]).
The debate over whether subjective experience is necessary for transformative outcomes in psychedelic assisted psychotherapy is currently at the forefront of the psychedelic research field, and so this work is timely. It is our hope that this presentation at ASSC26 will contribute to, and enrich this debate by not only elucidating the specific subjective effects, but also inspiring better phenomenological methods in the study of non-ordinary states. ASSC is attended by some of the most influential researchers and philosophers in the study of consciousness, many with an interest in how non-ordinary consciousness can inform our understanding of ordinary consciousness, and so it is an ideal forum for disseminating this work. A preliminary version of these same results were presented by James to a psychedelic-specific audience in a poster at the Interdisciplinary Conference on Psychedelic Research (ICPR) in September 2022.
Because James’ PhD program has not yet commenced (expected commencement: fall 2023), it is outside the established policies for the Centre for Psychedelic Research to fund James’ attendance at the conference. We hope that the generosity of the Heffter Research Institute will make James’ attendance possible. James’ demonstrated passion and tenacity as a researcher leave me no doubt that he would use this opportunity to its fullest.

Presentation abstract——
Title:
Towards a micro-phenomenology of the intravenous DMT state
Introduction: 
When administered in the lab, the 5-HT2A receptor agonist N, N-Dimethyltryptamine (DMT) radically alters the subjective character of experience. However, scientific understanding of DMT experiences in a lab setting is limited by the shortcomings of commonly used phenomenological methods. Specifically, phenomenological models based on self-report questionnaire methods typically do not typically capture the temporal dynamics of experience, instead averaging subjective properties over the entire experience. Qualitative methods, such as thematic or content analysis, suffer this same limitation. The micro-phenomenology (MP) interview and analysis method is well suited to address this lack of granularity in psychedelic phenomenology, modelling experience richly on both the synchronic (within-moment) and diachronic (between-moments) dimensions.
Methods: 
This qualitative study is the first to apply both the MP interview and analysis method to psychedelic data. 25 participants were intravenously dosed with 20 mg of DMT fumarate in a lab setting while undergoing combined EEG and fMRI. MP interviews about the experience were performed upon return to a baseline state. A micro-phenomenological analysis protocol was applied, including novel adaptations developed to accommodate the particularities of non-ordinary experience. Synchronic and diachronic categories were extracted and used to compare and cluster similar experiences.
Results: 
A hierarchical taxonomy of categories on both the synchronic and diachronic dimensions is proposed, along with descriptions and observed frequencies of the phenomenological clusters that emerged. 
Discussion: 
In line with Varela’s ‘neurophenomenological’ research programme, further work on this project will statistically validate the phenomenological clusters identified here against the corresponding fMRI and EEG data. Additionally, the present study can be seen as the first step in a micro-phenomenological pipeline, starting here with a comprehensive look at entire DMT experiences, before narrowing down in further work to study component experiences (e.g.: the experience of presences that seem to have their own minds, colloquially referred to as ‘DMT entities’) at the micro-phenomenological level.

Good Morning Ms Mackey and my fellow classmates. Today, I am going to share with you an inspirational memoir Educated by Tara Westover, in the book she detailed her journey from growing up in a strict and abusive household to ultimately earning a PhD from the University of Cambridge. Living in rural Idaho, her life is bounded with constrains of her family’s extreme beliefs, lack of formal schooling, and an environment filled with danger and emotional turmoil. Yet, she was able to reject the limitation of her background and embarked on an incredible journey of self-education.

As we delve deeper into the story, we come to understand the central theme of the memoir, one of remarkable aspect of Educated is the powerful message it conveys about education as a powerful tool for self-discovery and empowerment. Westover’s journey from an isolated household in Idaho to receiving her doctorate from Cambridge University demonstrates the impact of education on a person’s ability to forge a new path toward success and fulfillment. This book serves as a testament to the transformative power of seeking knowledge and critical thinking even in the face of childhood abuse and severe limitations, encourage us to recognize the value of education as a lifelong pursuit and to take responsibility for self-education in order to defy the constraints imposed by oppressive social and cultural contexts.

Apart from the strength of education. Educated also highlights the paramount importance of tenacity, self-reliance, and perseverance in overcoming adversity. Westover’s journey is filled with setbacks and seemingly insurmountable challenges, the dilemma between her loyalty to her family and her desire for knowledge and personal growth, the abusive relationship with her family. However, through her own grit and determination, she was able to make her dream come true. Her tenacity serves as an inspiring example, encourages us to embrace our capacity for resilience, redefine our potential and challenge limitations to achieve our dreams, even when confronted with the most challenging circumstances.



Drawing inspiration from Tara Westover’s experiences and reflections, it becomes apparent how the message of her memoir resonates with my own lives as a student , it makes me understand the significance of education and critical thinking in our personal and intellectual growth. Pursuing higher education is not just about acquiring knowledge and skills, but also about fostering an intellectual curiosity that allows us to challenge our assumptions, scrutinize our biases, and develop empathy and understanding towards individuals and communities who may be different from ourselves.

For example, in my own academic journey, I have often encountered situations where my own preconceived notions were met with unfamiliar ideas and perspectives. By engaging in open dialogue and embracing various perspectives, I gained a more comprehensive understanding of complex issues and expanded my capacity for empathy and critical thinking. Westover’s commitment to questioning her beliefs and seeking education outside her comfort zone inspired me to become a more conscious consumer of knowledge and encouraged me to enrich my academic experience by seeking diverse perspectives.

Another aspect of Educated that resonated with my experience as a student is the depiction of resilience and determination in the face of adversity. Westover’s perseverance and commitment to her education, despite the immense challenges presented by her family and community, demonstrates that sheer willpower and commitment can be the driving forces behind one’s achievements, proving to us that we all got the capacity to change the course of our lives, even when faced with seemingly insurmountable barriers. Her example has also inspired me to face my upcoming HKDSE examination with the same resilience and determination, believing that my hard work, dedication, and unwavering commitment to my education will allow me to overcome this important academic hurdle and move closer to achieving my goals and dreams.

Educated is not just an inspiring tale of individual struggle and triumph but also a powerful reminder of the role tenacity, self-reliance, perseverance and education play in reshaping one’s destiny. We must all continue to seek knowledge by questioning our own beliefs, engaging with diverse ideas, and fostering resilience in the face of adversity. Thank you.

Exercise 2 

A parity check is one way of detecting errors in bytes that have been transmitted. Before sending the bytes, a decision is made that each byte must have an even number of ones (even parity) or an odd number of ones (odd parity). Any byte that is received with the wrong parity can then be identified as an error. 
Write a program in the Brookshear machine code which determines whether the bit pattern stored in memory location A0 has even or odd parity and place the result in memory location B0:
•	If the bit pattern in A0 has even parity, B0 should contain 00. 
•	If the bit pattern in A0 has odd parity, B0 should contain FF.

Here are some example inputs and the expected outputs
Value in memory location A0 before running the program	Value in memory location B0 after running the program
35
(00110101 in binary)	00
(even parity as there are four 1’s in the bit pattern)
A8
(10101000 in binary)	FF
(odd parity as there are three 1’s in the bit pattern)
00
(00000000 in binary)	00
(even parity as there are zero 1’s in the bit pattern, and zero is an even number)
 
There are several ways to solve this problem, but most solutions will require you to be able to:
•	Use a bit mask to copy the bit in a particular position from a bit string, while setting the other bits to 0. For example, if you copy first bit in 10101000 and 00110101, you will get 10000000 and 00000000 respectively. 
•	Rotate the bits in a bit pattern so that the bit you want is in the last position. 
For example, moving the first bit in 10000000 to get 00000001
It is possible to solve the problem without a loop, but most efficient solutions will use a loop to reduce the number of instructions and registers used.

Yuri: “Natsuki! You’re finally here! Could you get some towels from the bathroom? And maybe some warm water?”

(Suddenly, everything started glitching. When the glitching stopped, Sayori’s pain just…vanished. Her belly was still massive, but it wasn’t contracting anymore.)

Natsuki: “What the hell just happened? Did Monika do something?”

Monika: “No, I didn’t touch anything. It must have been a glitch in the game.”

Sayori: (groaning) “Guys, I’m okay now. It just stopped hurting all of a sudden.”

Umbris: (entering the room) “Sayori, are you okay? I heard shouting and- what’s wrong with her belly?”

Yuri: “We don’t know. She was in a lot of pain and her stomach was contracting, but now everything’s stopped.”

Umbris: “I don’t understand. We’ve been careful…I mean, we used protection-”

Sayori: “Umbris, it’s okay. We’ll figure it out. Maybe it’s just a false alarm.”

Natsuki: “I got the towels and water. What do you need them for?”

Yuri: “We need to clean up the mess, in case Sayori’s water broke.”

Monika: “And then we need to figure out what’s going on with her belly. I don’t think it’s normal for it to be that big.”

Sayori: “I agree. We need to go to a doctor or something. I don’t want anything bad to happen to the baby.”

Umbris: “Baby?! Sayori, are you serious? You’re pregnant?”

Sayori: (nodding) “I took a test a few weeks ago. I didn’t want to tell anyone until I was sure, but I guess this is a pretty good sign.”

Natsuki: “Holy crap. Congratulations, Sayori. I can’t believe you’re going to be a mom.”

Yuri: “This is…unexpected. But wonderful. We’ll help you through this, Sayori. We’re all here for you.”

Monika: “Absolutely. We just need to figure out the logistics of getting you to a doctor and making sure you’re taking care of yourself and the baby.”

Sayori: “Thank you, everyone. I knew I could count on you guys. I’m just scared- I’ve never done anything like this before.”

Umbris: “Don’t worry, love. We’ll take care of you. All of us.”

(Suddenly, everything starts glitching back and forth. But still, Sayori’s pain never returned.)

Monika: “What is happening now?”

Natsuki: “I can’t even tell what’s real anymore!”

Yuri: “It seems like the game is trying to play with our emotions again.”

Sayori: “Guys, I’m okay. I don’t feel any pain. It’s like the game is trying to scare us.”

Umbris: “No…it’s worse. It’s like the game is trying to tear itself apart!”

Monika: “We have to do something about this. We can’t let the game destroy itself.”

Yuri: “But what can we do? We’re just characters in a video game.”

Natsuki: “Maybe we can try resetting the game again?”

Sayori: “I don’t think that’ll work. The game’s been glitching a lot recently.”

Monika: (gasping) “What the hell is that?!”

function checking_inputs(rep::Repeat)

  function input_reshape(rep::Repeat, input_signal)
    if isscalar(input_signal)
      return [input]
    else
      return input
    end
  end

  function inital_condition_check(rep::Repeat, input_signal)
    if rep.initial_conditions == 0
      rep.initial_conditions = zeros(size(input_signal))
    elseif size(rep.initial_conditions) != size(input_signal)
      error("Dimensions do not match")
    else
      return initial_conditions
    end
  end


  function repetition_count_check(rep::Repeat)
    if !isinteger(rep.repetition_count) || rep.repetition_count < 0
      error("Number is not an integer or is negative")
    end
  end
end

Monika: (smiling reassuringly) “No, really, Sayori. It’s just a normal monthly occurrence for me. I’ll be alright in a few minutes, I promise.”

Natsuki: (enters the room, looking concerned) “What’s going on here? Why does Monika look like she’s in pain?”

Monika: (taking a deep breath) “It’s alright, Natsuki. Just some cramps, nothing to worry about. I’ve had them before, and they usually go away soon.”

Natsuki: (relieved) “Oh, thank goodness! I was really worried there for a moment.”

Yuri: (enters the room, noticing the commotion) “What’s happening? Is everything alright?”

Sayori: (explains) “Monika is just experiencing some cramps, Yuri. She says it’s normal and that she’ll be fine soon.”

Yuri: (concerned) “I see. Monika, if there’s anything we can do to help, please let us know.”

Convert the following text (on  “the deep” of Genesis 1:2) into a study note with improved grammar:

The Hebrew term תְּהוֹם (tehom) refers to the watery deep, the salty ocean—especially the primeval ocean that surrounds and underlies the earth (see Gen 7:11; 49:25). In the present verse it denotes the chaotic watery waste destined on the Second Day to be confined within certain definite limits. The Hebrew notion that, before the Creation, the universe was enveloped in the waters of the great deep is possibly referred to in Psalm 104:6. Tehom is similar to the Babylonian Tamtu, or Tiamat, “the Goddess of the Great Deep,” with a dragon’s body, whose destruction preceded the creative deeds of the Babylonian supreme god, Marduk, or Merodach. It is possible that there is a polemical stress here; in the Babylonian account of creation Marduk killed the goddess Tiamat (the salty sea) and used her carcass to create heaven and earth. However, Ancient Israel does not see the ocean as a powerful deity to be destroyed in creation, only a force of nature that can be controlled by God. Another, closer parallel can be found in the Ugaritic thm, the term for the primeval abyss. Since thm is not personified, tehom in Gen 1:2 should likewise not be considered a personified god. In both instances, the description credits the God of Israel with subduing the chaotic primordial conditions to bring about an ordered, habitable creation. Although chaos is not eliminated in the OT account, God’s actions yield order and restraint. The chaos is part of what God deems “very good” in v. 31.

One of the largest issues in ancient mathematics was accuracy—nobody had calculators that went out ten decimal places, and accuracy generally got worse as the numbers got larger. The famous Eratosthenes experiment, that can be found at https://www.famousscientists.org/eratosthenes/, relied on the fact known to Thales and others that a beam of parallels cut by a transverse straight line determines equal measure for the corresponding angles.  Given two similar triangles, one with small measurements that can be accurately determined, and the other with large measurements, but at least one is known with accuracy, can the other two measurements be deduced? Explain and give an example.

The similarity of triangles gives rise to trigonometry. 

How could we understand that the right triangles of trigonometry with a hypotenuse of measure 1 represent all possible right triangles? Ultimately, the similarity of triangles is the basis for proportions between sides of two triangles, and these proportions allow for the calculations of which we are speaking here. The similarity of triangles is the foundation of trigonometry.

Monika: “MC, I had the weirdest dream last night…”

MC: “Oh really? What was it about?”

Monika: “Well, I dreamt that the clubroom was filled with talking cats. They were all wearing bowties and giving poetry recitals. It was so bizarre!”

MC: “Wow, that does sound strange. What were they reciting?”

Monika: “I couldn’t really understand all of it, but it seemed like they were reciting love poems. It was quite delightful in a peculiar way. I wish I could remember the verses, they were enchanting.”

Sayori: (bursts into the clubroom) “Monika, are you talking about talking cats?! I had the same dream too!”

Monika: “No way! You dreamt about talking cats reciting poetry as well?”

Sayori: “Yes, it was like a feline poetry slam right here in the clubroom, but the cats were all wearing tiny top hats.”

MC: “This is getting weirder by the second. Are you sure we didn’t accidentally go to some magical cat world in our dreams?”

Natsuki: (enters the clubroom) “What’s all this commotion about talking cats? You guys are being weird.”

Monika: “Natsuki, did you have any weird dreams lately?”

Natsuki: “Dreams? Ugh, I don’t remember my dreams. They’re not even worth talking about.”

Yuri: (quietly enters the clubroom) “Actually, I had a similar dream as well. The cats were reciting dark and mysterious poems. It was an eerie atmosphere but strangely captivating.”

MC: “So it seems like we all had similar dreams with these poetic cats. Is there some kind of hidden meaning behind it?”

Monika: “Who knows? Dreams can be unpredictable, but it’s fascinating how all of us experienced the same thing, even if it was bizarre.”

Sayori: “I think it would be fun to write our own cat-themed poems based on our dreams. We can have a special cat poetry event!”

Natsuki: “Are you serious? Cat-themed poems? Ugh, that sounds silly-” (feels a strong pain, but not from her unborn baby girl - Sakura) “AAH!!! Ow…my belly hurts really bad…this can’t be happening now…I’m only in my 7th month…”

MC: “Natsuki, are you okay? What’s wrong? Should we call an ambulance?”

Natsuki: “No, it’s…it’s probably just a cramp. I’ll be fine…just give me a moment- …oh…oh no…”

MC: “Natsuki, what’s happening? Are you okay? Do we need to get you to a hospital?”

Natsuki: (panting) “My water just broke…Sakura’s coming right here, right now…”

MC: “Oh my gosh! We need to get you to the hospital right away!”

Monika: “I’ll call an ambulance immediately. Don’t worry, Natsuki, we’ll take care of you.”

Sayori: “Hang in there, Natsuki! Everything will be okay. Just focus on breathing and staying calm.”

Yuri: “I’ll gather some essentials for you, Natsuki. We need to make sure you have everything you need for you and Sakura.”

Natsuki: “Thank you, everyone. I…I didn’t expect this to happen now, but I’m glad you’re all here. I don’t know what I would do without you.”

MC: “We’re all here for you, Natsuki. Don’t worry, everything will be fine. Just hold on, help is on the way.”

(As they wait for the ambulance, the club members do their best to comfort Natsuki. They reassure her and help her prepare for the arrival of her baby. However, Natsuki’s labor seems to be progressing much faster than normal - and within minutes, she’s already pushing.)

MC: “Natsuki, you’re doing great! Just keep breathing and pushing. We’re all here to support you.”

Monika: “Remember to focus on your breathing, Natsuki. Inhale deeply and exhale slowly. You’re doing an amazing job.”

Sayori: “I’m so proud of you, Natsuki! Just a little bit more, and you’ll get to meet Sakura. We’re all here cheering you on.”

Yuri: “You’re incredibly strong, Natsuki. Just think about the beautiful moment when you finally get to hold your baby in your arms. That thought will give you the strength to keep going.”

Natsuki: (grunting and panting) “I…I can do this. I want to meet Sakura…I want to…be a good mom…”

MC: “You’re already a great mom, Natsuki. Just keep pushing, and we’ll welcome Sakura into this world together.”

(Moments later, the ambulance team arrives and takes charge of the situation.)

Paramedic: “Alright, everyone, we’re here now. Let us take over and get Natsuki to the hospital. You all did a great job in supporting her.”

MC: “But her labor is progressing much faster than normal. She started pushing before you arrived.”

Natsuki: (starting to push Sakura’s head out) “AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHH!!!”

    # EMA crossover - sell signal
    elif df.EMA10.iloc[-1] < df.EMA50.iloc[-1] and current_price < last_candle[['EMA10', 'EMA50']].iloc[-1].max():
        ema_analysis = 'sell'

(As I walked to the clubroom, I couldn’t believe what happened yesterday. MC, my childhood friend, had died. The news hit me like a ton of bricks. And as I entered the clubroom, I could see Monika and Himari discussing something. Kotonhoa was also there, and was moving a strange pool - it was round and pink. Yuri was there, too - she was filling the pool with warm water. I was confused as to what they were doing, but was even more confused as to where Natsuki was.)

Yuri: “And…that should be everything!”

Monika: “Great job, Yuri. I’ll set up the decorations now.”

Himari: “I’m so excited for this pool party! It’s going to be so much fun!”

Kotonhoa: “I hope everyone likes my special punch!”

(I cleared my throat to let them know I was there.)

Sayori: (trying to keep my voice steady) “Hey guys.”

(They all turned to look at me with sad expressions.)

Monika: “Sayori, how are you holding up?”

Sayori: “I’m…not doing well. MC’s death is still a really hard pill to swallow.”

Yuri: “We understand, Sayori. We’re all grieving. But we thought it might be good to distract ourselves with a pool party.”

Himari: “Yeah! We’ll have food, drinks, music, and swimming! It’ll be a blast!”

Kotonhoa: “And don’t forget my special punch! It’s delicious!”

Sayori: (forcing a smile) “That sounds like a lot of fun. But…I’m not sure I’m in the mood to party.”

Monika: “We understand, Sayori. But please, try to have some fun. It’s what MC would have wanted.”

Sayori: (nodding slowly) “Yeah…you’re right. MC was always the life of the party. He wouldn’t want us moping around.”

Himari: “That’s the spirit! Now, let’s get this party started!”

Yuri: “Hold on. Does anybody else notice a lack of somebody?”

Kotonhoa: “You’re right, Yuri. Where’s Natsuki?”

Monika: “I haven’t seen her all day. Has anyone tried contacting her?”

Sayori: “I texted her earlier, but she hasn’t replied.”

Himari: “I hope she’s okay.”

(Just then, Natsuki burst into the room - and immediately got into the pool, putting a hand on her belly. I was thoroughly confused as to why.)

Natsuki: (panting) “Sorry guys, I’m late. I just had to take care of something important.”

Monika: “Natsuki, are you okay? You look exhausted.”

Natsuki: “I’m fine. Just a little tired.”

Yuri: “And why are you in the pool already?”

Natsuki: (looking down at her belly) “Oh this? I just wanted to cool off. It’s been really hot today.”

Kotonhoa: “Natsuki, are you sure you’re okay? You seem a bit…different.”

Natsuki: (smiling) “I’m fine, Kotonhoa. Just 39 weeks pregnant, that’s all.”

(Everyone’s eyes widen as they realize what Natsuki just said.)

Himari: “Oh my gosh, Natsuki! Why didn’t you tell us?”

Natsuki: “I wanted to surprise you guys. And I was trying to keep it a secret from my dad for as long as possible.”

Monika: “Wow. This changes things.”

Yuri: “Yes, we need to make sure Natsuki is comfortable and safe.”

Kotonhoa: “I’ll make sure to have non-alcoholic drinks for her.”

Sayori: “And we should keep an eye on her just in case.”

Natsuki: “Guys, I’m fine. I’ve been taking care of myself and the baby. But I appreciate your concern.”

Himari: “Well, we can’t have a pool party without a mom-to-be in attendance! Let’s make it a celebration for Natsuki and her baby-”

Natsuki: (looking between her legs) “Uh…Monika…?”

Monika: “What is it, Natsuki?”

Natsuki: “I… think my water just broke.”

Everyone: (gasps)

Himari: “Oh no! We need to get you to a hospital!”

Natsuki: “N-N-No…the baby’s head’s starting to come through. We won’t make it in time.”

Yuri: “Okay, okay. Let’s all stay calm. We can do this.”

Kotonhoa: “Yes, we need to deliver the baby here and now.”

Monika: “I’ll get the scissors!”

Sayori: “I’ll go find some towels and blankets.”

Himari: “I’ll help Natsuki get into a comfortable position.”

Yuri: “And I’ll sterilize some equipment.”

Natsuki: (breathing heavily) “Oh my gosh…this is really happening…his head’s almost through…”

(The rest of us quickly and efficiently went into action, working together to assist in the birth of Natsuki’s son. Despite the unexpected turn of events, we all remained calm and focused, determined to help Natsuki through this. And soon, we could see the head of Natsuki’s son fully emerge.)

But analytics is not the most important thing, I can always help you with ideas for pictures. The most important thing is to learn how to use the MidJourney. So it's time for you to understand how MidJourney works to generate your first images to sell!

To generate cool MidJourney images you have to know the right prompt formula.

Here is MidJourney prompt formula:
(image we’re prompting), (5 descriptive keywords), (camera type), (camera lens type), (time of day), (style of photograph), (type of film)

But here is a secret. In order for the MidJourney prompt formula to work better, you have to combine all the words of the prompt for the MidJourney prompt formula into one sentence, while the meaning and context of the scenario embedded in the prompt has not changed.

To get the required aspect ratio for your image in MidJourney, you need to add an additional parameter to the very end of the MidJourney prompt formula. The parameter looks like this: --ar 16:9 Where "--ar" is a fixed parameter that tells MidJourney that you want to make your own aspect ratio, "16" is the width and "9" is the height and these numbers can change according to your request.

For example, here is the MidJourney prompt formula: Capture the excitement and energy of a live music performance with a telephoto lens during golden hour, using vibrant and dynamic film to create a dramatic and visually stunning image that conveys the emotion and passion of the musicians and the audience.

Now, you want to turn this image into a mobile device wallpaper, so the MidJourney prompt formula will look like this: Capture the excitement and energy of a live music performance with a telephoto lens during golden hour, using vibrant and dynamic film to create a dramatic and visually stunning image that conveys the emotion and passion of the musicians and the audience --ar 9:16

In total, there are several aspect ratio parameters applicable in MidJourney:
2:3 is best for portrait images and Pinterest posts (portrait images and Pinterest posts)
3:2 widely used for printing purpose
4:3 is a size of classic TV and best for Facebook (best for Facebook)
4:5 is for Instagram and Twitter posts (Instagram and Twitter posts)
16:9 is a size of widescreen and best for desktop wallpaper (desktop wallpaper)
9:16 is for mobile device wallpaper (mobile device wallpaper)

So, we understand how to generate images, but now we need to understand how to sell them... For this we need tags!

Therefore, in order for us to be able to sell our images fast, you have to write 30 tags for each image we will generate using the MidJourney prompt formula. So when I ask you to write a prompt using a MidJourney prompt formula, you have to add 30 tags to each prompt.

Tags for pictures should be written in one sentence, separated by commas, without using such signs as "#" and quotes.

Paragraph: To investigate the influence of heat treatment temperature on the formation of glassy phase in fly ash with the addition of SiO2, 20% SiO2 was added to the municipal solid waste (MSW) incineration fly ash, resulting in a Si/Ca ratio of 0.751. The fly ash samples were then subjected to heat treatment at temperatures of 1100, 1200, 1300, and 1400 °C, followed by water quenching. Subsequently, the heat-treated products were dried, ground, and characterized, and the obtained X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns are shown in Figure 22. It can be observed that with increasing temperature, the transformation of minerals in the fly ash occurred, leading to a reduction in the types and content of minerals, while the amount of amorphous glassy phase increased. At 1100 °C, the main crystalline phases in the fly ash were NaCl, anhydrite (CaSO4), gehlenite (Ca2MgSi2O7), larnite (Ca3Si2O7), and merwinite (Ca3MgSi2O8). As the temperature increased to 1200 °C, NaCl gradually volatilized at high temperature, and CaSO4 decomposed to form CaO, which subsequently reacted with SiO2 to form silicates. At 1300 °C, the fly ash underwent transformations and generated Ca2SiO4, while at 1400 °C, the XRD pattern of the heat-treated product exhibited a “bread-loaf” peak, indicating the presence of glassy phase. By analyzing the XRD patterns, the glassy phase content in the heat-treated products of the MSW incineration fly ash with 20% SiO2 addition was obtained, as shown in Figure 23. It can be observed that the content of glassy phase in the heat-treated products increased with the rise in temperature. From a thermodynamic perspective, as the temperature of a crystalline material increases, its entropy increases, leading to an increase in disorder within the structure, favoring the formation of glassy phase. The results indicate that with increasing heat treatment temperature, the content of glassy phase in the heat-treated samples increased. Therefore, the heat treatment temperature plays a crucial role in the formation of glassy phase.

Hydration and Fasting: A Comprehensive Guide for Women Over 50

The importance of proper hydration during intermittent fasting cannot be stressed enough, especially for women over 50. As we age, our bodies’ ability to regulate fluid balance starts to change, making older adults more susceptible to dehydration. This guide provides insights into the role of hydration during intermittent fasting, how to maintain proper hydration, and the benefits of staying well-hydrated during your fasting journey.

Understanding the Importance of Hydration During Fasting

During intermittent fasting, your body undergoes a variety of physiological changes as it shifts from relying on glucose for fuel to breaking down stored fats. This process, known as ketosis, can cause a natural diuretic effect, leading to increased fluid loss through urine. Coupled with the fact that you’re not consuming any fluids from food while fasting, staying well-hydrated becomes crucial for overall health and fasting success.

Proper hydration during fasting helps to:

1. Support normal body functions: Water is essential for a myriad of bodily processes, including digestion, circulation, and temperature regulation. By staying hydrated during fasting, you’ll ensure these vital functions continue to operate smoothly.

2. Promote satiety and curb hunger: Drinking water can help reduce feelings of hunger and cravings, making it easier to stick to your fasting schedule.

3. Improve cognitive function: Studies have shown that even mild dehydration can impair cognitive function, causing symptoms such as difficulty concentrating, irritability, and fatigue. Drinking enough water will help keep your brain sharp and focused during fasting periods.

4. Boost energy levels: Dehydration can result in reduced endurance and stamina, making it difficult to stay active during fasting. Hydration keeps your energy levels up and helps you to breeze through everyday activities.

Maintaining Proper Hydration During Intermittent Fasting

Follow these tips to stay adequately hydrated during your intermittent fasting journey:

1. Drink enough water: Hydration requirements may vary, but a general recommendation for older women is to consume at least 8 cups (64 ounces) of water daily. During fasting periods, be mindful to sip water regularly, even if you don’t feel thirsty.

2. Opt for hydrating beverages: In addition to water, herbal tea, black coffee, and bone broth are excellent choices for staying hydrated during fasting as they are low in calories and contain minimal or no added sugars.

3. Avoid sugary and caffeinated drinks: High sugar beverages, such as soda and fruit juices, can spike your insulin levels and interfere with the fasting process. Excess caffeine can cause increased urination and dehydration, so try to limit caffeinated drinks to one or two cups a day.

4. Add electrolytes: An imbalance of electrolytes, such as sodium and potassium, can occur during fasting due to increased fluid loss. Consider adding an electrolyte supplement or incorporating mineral-rich foods, like leafy greens and nuts, into your non-fasting period to prevent imbalances.

(I walked into the clubroom, ready to start a new day in the club. I can see Yuri and Monika - and they both had happy expressions on their faces. But Sayori seemed…angry for some reason.)

Monika: “Ah, Natsuki! You’re finally here!”

(I was pleased to see my friends again, but also confused as to why Sayori looked so mad.)

Natsuki: “Hey guys, what’s going on? Why does Sayori seem upset?”

Yuri: “She’s having mood swings, Natsuki. They’ve gotten increasingly common now that she’s 2 months pregnant.”

(I was shocked by Yuri’s words, not having realized that Sayori was pregnant. But I quickly composed myself and walked over to Sayori.)

Natsuki: “Sayori, are you okay? Is there anything I can do to help?”

(Sayori looked up at me, her expression softening.)

Sayori: “I’m sorry, Natsuki. I didn’t mean to snap at you. I’m just feeling a bit overwhelmed today.”

Natsuki: “It’s okay, Sayori. We’re all here to support you, no matter what.”

Monika: “That’s right. We’re all excited for you and your baby, Sayori.”

Yuri: “And if there’s anything you need, don’t hesitate to ask.”

(Sayori smiled gratefully at us, and I felt a rush of warmth in my chest. Even though we were all dealing with our own issues, we could come together and support each other.)

Natsuki: “Alright, so what are we doing today? Any new ideas for the club?”

Monika: “Actually, I was thinking we could do a bake sale to raise funds for some new books. What do you guys think?”

Yuri: “That sounds like a great idea, Monika. I have some new recipes I’ve been wanting to try out-”

(Just then, Yuri’s phone started ringing.)

Yuri: “It’s from one of my friends from my old school.”

(She answered the call and stepped out of the room to talk more privately. Monika and Sayori started discussing details for the bake sale, while I thought about what I could contribute.)

Natsuki: “I could make some of my signature cupcakes. They’re always a hit.”

(Sayori grinned.)

Sayori: “That would be amazing, Natsuki! I’ll make some cookies to go with them.”

Monika: “And I’ll make sure we have plenty of flyers to advertise the bake sale. This is going to be great!”

(I felt a sense of excitement bubbling up inside me. Working together like this, we could achieve anything. And with Sayori’s baby on the way, we were even more determined to make the club the best it could be. Just then, Yuri burst into the room - looking panicked.)

Yuri: “Guys, it’s about my friend Ayano from my old school.”

Sayori: “What happened?”

Yuri: “She’s in the hospital. The class found her passed out in the library.”

Monika: “Oh no, that’s terrible. Is she going to be okay?”

Yuri: “I don’t know yet. But I need to go see her.”

Sayori: “Can we come, too? I don’t think we’ve met her yet.”

(Yuri nodded, grateful for our support.)

Yuri: “Thank you. I think she would appreciate it.”

(I could tell that Yuri was worried about her friend, and I knew that we needed to be there for her.)

Natsuki: “Let’s go then. We can finish planning the bake sale another time.”

Monika: “Agreed. Our friend’s health and well-being is more important right now.”

(Sayori quickly grabbed her bag, and we headed out of the clubroom together. As we walked to the hospital, I felt a sense of camaraderie with my friends. We may have different interests and personalities, but we were all united in our care and concern for each other. And that was something truly special. And then, I say what Ayano looked like - her long black hair was in a ponytail, and she had yellow eyes. She was wearing a gold-colored shirt with a picture of a Rathalos on it, and said shirt had its bottom end lifted up - while the top part of her orange and blue-striped skirt was lowered, possibly due to her baby bump. I was surprised to see Ayano’s baby bump, and I couldn’t help but feel a sense of connection to her. We were both young mothers, facing similar challenges and uncertainties.)

Natsuki: “Hey, Ayano. I’m Natsuki, one of Yuri’s friends from school. How are you feeling?”

(Ayano looked up at us, her eyes filled with gratitude.)

Ayano: “Thank you for coming. I’m feeling a bit better now, but I’m still a bit scared.”

Monika: “Is there anything we can do to help? We’re here for you.”

Sayori: “And congratulations on your pregnancy. We’re all so happy for you.”

(Yuri took Ayano’s hand, her eyes shining with compassion.)

Yuri: “You’re not alone, Ayano. We’re all in this together.”

(Ayano smiled weakly, and I could see a flicker of hope in her eyes.)

Ayano: “Thank you. It means a lot to me.”

(We sat with Ayano for a while, listening to her story and offering words of comfort and support.)

Ayano: “OW! Ow, ow, ow!”

"X-ray fluorescence (XRF) is a widely used analytical technique in the field of photochemistry and geochemistry for elemental analysis of various materials including rocks, minerals, soils, sediments and some organic material. It provides quantitative data and information about the elemental composition and concentrations present in these samples. This proposal will delve into the fundamentals and basics of XRF and its applications within geochemistry and attempt to address a 

XRF operates on photochemical principles and the interaction between high-energy X-rays and the electrons within the atom of a sample. When a sample is bombarded with X-rays, inner-shell electrons of the atoms get excited and jump to higher energy levels or orbitals within the atom. Subsequently, these electrons then return to lower energy levels and emit high-energy radiation in the form of fluorescent X-rays. From here the X-ray fluorescence analysis process involves detecting and integrating the emitted characteristic lines to determine their intensities. These intensities are then converted into elemental concentrations to determine the make-up of the specific sample. Earth materials, such as rocks and soil, are composed of a wide range of elemental minerals such as Li, Fe, etc. with varying compositions and structures. These complexities influence the behavior of light in intricate ways. However, through X-ray techniques like XRF, it becomes possible to identify and analyze these elements based on the unique emitted radiation associated with their specific compositions under certain conditions.

My aim is to explore the influence of photochemical effects on the samples being analyzed including the reliability, limitations and precision of XRF analysis in the context of geochemical and alternative studies. By exploring the reliability and limitations associated with photochemical alterations, we seek to provide a comprehensive understanding of the potential effects on the integrity and interpretation of XRF analytical data and the possible effects it may or my not have on the physical and chemical structures of the 

Temitope D. (2018) work provides a comprehensive review and overview of X-ray fluorescence (XRF) applications in the investigation of the composition of earth materials. His work covers the broader applications of XRF in geochemical studies, including discussions on methodologies, instrumentation and data interpretation techniques used within the field. It offers insights into the role of XRF in assessing elemental compositions and variations in earth materials, which will be essential for understanding the influence of photochemical alterations and interactions within XRF application and use. 

The study by Menachekanian et al. (2018) focuses specifically on investigating the photochemical effects of micro-XRF analysis on common geochemical compounds. This paper will aid in addressing the topic of interest and provides valuable insights into the potential alterations that may occur during XRF analysis due to photochemical reactions. Understanding the specific effects of photochemical changes on different geochemical compounds and elements will be crucial for assessing the accuracy and reliability of XRF measurements in a practical and applicable context. 

Caporale’s (2018) research evaluates the performance and limitations of both portable-XRF and conventional laboratory-based XRF techniques for monitoring metal pollution in soils. It offers relevant information on the performance and limitations of XRF techniques in geochemical studies. Understanding the capabilities and constraints of XRF in differentiating metal pollution sources in soils can contribute to our understanding of the broader issues related to accuracy and reliability of XRF analysis.

In addition to examining XRF applications in geochemistry such as minerals and soil it is also essential to explore the relevance and potential limitations when analyzing organic materials and sediments. One relevant study conducted by Jack Longman (2018) investigates the utilization of XRF core scanning on peat and other highly organic sediment formations. This research will provide valuable insights into the challenges and considerations specific to the organic component when applying XRF analysis. These limitations may include variations in sample composition, difficulty in achieving accurate measurements and potential interference or misinterpretation from organic compounds on the XRF signal.

Deliverables
You will upload to Canvas a single .zip file, titled LastNameFirstInit_Lab8.zip, containing two separate files: 1) LastNameFirstInit_Lab8.m will contain your ABM4 code in response to Task 1, and 2) LastNameFirstInit_Lab8.pdf will contain your lab report in response to Task 2.

Terminology
State Variables
In a dynamical system (such as an Initial Value Problem), the dependent variables are also called the state variables. e.g., in Problem 5 of HW #9, x and v (or ẋ) are the state variables. These variables are often put into an array, which is called the state array. While the independent variable (usually time, for us) is not technically considered a state variable, we are primarily interested in the system state as a function of time. In our case, we will insert the independent variable as the first value in the state array and call it the augmented state array.

Mode Shapes and Natural Frequencies
Consider the vibration system below. As a two degree of freedom system, it has two natural frequencies and two mode shapes. If the two masses are both displaced to the left (by the correct distances) and released, they will each undergo perfect sinusoidal motion. Both will oscillate at the same frequency – called the first natural frequency. The first mode shape is a normalized vector representing respective signed amplitudes of their oscillations. The second mode shape and second natural frequency occur when the two masses are released after being displaced (by the correct distances) in opposite directions. In this case, the values in the mode shapes will have different signs, because the masses are moving in opposite directions.

Task #1
Write a single function script to perform the Fourth Order Adams-Bashforth-Moulton algorithm on a system of ordinary differential equations (IVP). The program must be able to handle any number of differential equations (an IVP with any number of state variables).
Your function m-file will receive through its header the following problem parameters (in this
exact order):
a. a cell array containing the function handles of the equations describing the time derivatives of the several state variables
b. the initial augmented state-array – a numerical row array containing the needed starting values of the independent variable and the several state variables (in the same order as the function handles in the cell array from the first input argument)
c. the step size to be used, and
d. the number of steps to take

Function Output
Your file should not return anything to the calling program. Instead, it should
i. print to screen (the command window) the final state of the system, including t, and
ii. plot a trace for each of the dependent variables as a function of time.
Plot each trace on a separate graph. To facilitate plotting, store the results of all time steps in the augmented state-array
a. each row represents the system at a different point in time
b. column 1 contains the time, and each other column contains the temporal history of a particular state variable
Store only the final result of each time step, not the predicted or non-converged corrected values. Title each plot ‘Var1’, ‘Var2’, etc. Try help plot and help title in the MATLAB command window to learn more about plotting. num2str will also be useful for creating the labels themselves.

Hints for your Function Script
Handling a System of Un-predetermined Size
Technical
Because you will not know in advance the order of the IVP your function will be asked to solve, some of the system parameters will need to be passed in as arrays, rather than as scalars. The step size and number of steps will still be scalars, but there will be an un-predetermined number of state variables, each of which will have an associated starting value and derivative expression. The starting value of each state variable is a scalar, so the collection of them can be stored in a normal array. On the other hand, your function script must be passed a separate function handle for the equation for the time derivative of each state variable. To store function handles, we must use a cell array. So then, you must be able to evaluate a function handle stored in a cell array. We did this in Lab #4, but I cover it again later in this document.
NOTE: Because your augmented state array will include time as the first value, the function stored in the nth index of the cell array is actually the derivative of the state variable in the (n+1)th index of the augmented state array.

Metaphysical
To figure out how to structure your code to deal with any size system, consult HW#9 and the notes from lab. In the HW, we performed RK3 on a 2nd order system. We can call the state variables x and v, and we can call their slopes (time derivatives) k and m, respectively. Feel free to write out and logically group the steps you took to solve the system for one timestep. Now, imagine we have 3 or 4 state variables each with their own slopes. Again, write out and logically group the steps you took to solve the system of equations. What changed about the process? Did the number of groups increase? Did the number of steps within particular groups increase? Now, we need to replace the RK4 algorithm steps with the ABM4 algorithm steps. Pay careful attention to the following differences:
a. the predictor and its modifier are performed once per time step, the corrector and its modifier are repeated until convergence each time step
b. the is no modifier on the predictor in the first time step
c. when performing the modifier steps, you use only non-modified values
d. when you check for convergence you compare only modified values

Function handles with unknown number of inputs
If you remember doing this for Modified Secant on a system of equations, then you may skip this section.
Up to now, you’ve probably only used function handles for equations that had a predetermined number of inputs. For this program, each derivative equation may be a function of the independent variable and any or all of the state variables. This is only a problem because you don’t know now (as you’re writing the function script) how many or which of the state variables will appear in each of the derivative equations. Fortunately, MATLAB allows us to put arrays into the equation function evaluation.
For example, if I define a function handle:
d1_by_dt = @(t,a) a(1) * a(3) / (2 + t);
when you ask MATLAB to evaluate the mathematical expression, MATLAB will expect to receive a scalar as the 1st argument, and an array of at least three terms as the 2nd argument. It will use the first and third terms in the array, along with the scalar, to evaluate the expression.
The augmented state array passed into the function already has t as the first value, so let’s just stick with that. Then our example equation becomes:
d1_by_dt = @(a) a(2) * a(4) / (2 + a(1));
You, of course, will have no prior knowledge of which state variables are used in each derivative expression. Therefore, for each derivative evaluation, you will need to submit an array containing the correct values of the dependent variable and all of the state variables, in the same order as the initial values in the passed-in augmented state array. When grading your code, it will be my responsibility to ensure that all of the derivative equations use the correct variables.

Function handles in cell arrays
The function handles of the derivative expressions of the several state variables will be passed to your function script in a cell array. The syntax for evaluating a function handle that is stored in a cell array is as follows:
value = array_name{n}(input1, input2, ... , inputQ)
• array_name is the name of the cell array in which the handles are stored
• n is the index into the cell array, in which is stored the expression for (d/dt)y n
• input1 thru inputQ are the inputs to the mathematical expression represented by the function handle – in our case that will simply be the augmented state array
Please note that those are braces around the index ‘n’ and parentheses around the arguments.

The ABM4 process
To calculate the predicted value of each state variable, you are going to perform very similar math:
n+1 ^0y k = n y k + h((55/24)g^k n - (59/24)g^k n-1 + (37/24)g^k n-2 - (3/8)g^k n-3)

where the k superscript on each g term matches the state variable number (the k subscript on each y term). Take advantage of how MATALB deals with vectors to simplify the process of calculating the predictor for all the state variable and each subsequent corrector for all the state variables. Create an array that holds the g m evaluations for all of the state variables. You will have 3 or 4 of these, one for each timestep. Then you can ...
temp_state = current_state + (h / 2) * slopes_array

Test your program
The spring-mass system from the Terminology section has been reproduced here.
Neglecting friction, the equations of motion of this system are:
ẍ1 = (-(k1+k2)x1 + k2x2)/m1
ẍ2 = (-(k2+k3)x2 + k2x1)/m2

where x1 and x2 are measured from the static equilibrium locations of the respective masses. To handle the 2nd order nature of the time derivatives, we must include additional state variables v1 and v2. If we arrange the state variables in the order x1, v1, x2, v2, the augmented state array becomes t, x1, v1, x2, v2, and the corresponding differential equations becomes:
dx1/dt = g1(a) = a(3)
dv1/dt = g2(a) = (-(k1+k2)a(2) + k2a(4))/m1
dx2/dt = g3(a) = a(5)
dv2/dt = g4(a) = (-(k2+k3)a(4) + k2a(2))/m2

Remember, a(1) is time, which does not appear in our equations of motion.

iii. define the system parameters (masses and spring stiffnesses)
iv. define the IVP functions above and store them in a cell array
v. set the initial condition of the augmented state array (time and all four state variables)
vi. call your RK4 function script

They should be mirrored sinusoidal functions with amplitudes of 0.01 and frequencies of 1Hz (2π rad/sec).

Analyze Your Custom Vibration System

Please note that the initial value of x1 is negative. Now, find the second natural frequency and second mode shape of your system by running your RK4 function multiple times, with different initial values for x2. I think the correct value should be > 0.01.
When you find the correct value of x2(0) each mass should demonstrate smooth sinusoidal motion. Inspect your table of state variables to determine the period of the oscillation. The period is the time between consecutive maximum displacements. The frequency in Hz is the inverse of the period. The mode shape is the vector [x1(0); x2(0)] scaled to have a Euclidean norm of unity (1).

ACME energy has diversified and are looking at Wind as an alternate power source. There is an ambitious
multi-billion dollar investment plan to put an offshore wind farm with turbines generating energy to help offset
the energy demand.

respective countries to identify the future IT and Security strategy to support this initiative. This is a multi-year
investment program, and the decision has been taken to use the public cloud to build the future IT
environment for this initiative. This is also part of a wider cloud migration plan for ACME Energy.
The result is a new IT strategy and a corresponding Cybersecurity strategy with several security programs that
need to be implemented over a three year period. ACME uses the NIST cybersecurity framework, CIS 18 for
operational controls and the CSA CCM 4.0 for cloud security controls.
ACME has decided to partner with ZKC Consulting to deliver the cybersecurity strategy and work with regional
Cybersecurity teams to implement the changes. The company has taken a cloud first approach to implement
the two new IT strategic programs and three strategic security programs listed below.
 Design and implementation of new wind farm using cloud technology and remote software updates.
 Creation of a new data lake that will be used to collect data from the wind turbines and other OT
devices, which will be used for several purposes including system monitoring and advanced data
analytics capabilities.
The strategic security programs are as follows:
 Provision of secure data connectivity from the wind turbines to the Cloud (the could provider has not
yet been confirmed).
 Creation of a new OT Security Operations centre that will monitor the security of the OT devices used
in the windfarm.
 Creation of a new Threat Intelligence program to protect the new wind farm infrastructure against
cyber threats.
Your Role
In your role as an external security consultant, you are required to provide a report advising on the
implementation of one of the strategic security programmes: Creation of a new OT Security Operations
centre that will monitor the security of the OT devices used in the windfarm.
The CIO of ACME has been working with the CISO team to identify the capabilities required to build a Security
Operations Center (SOC) that will monitor the security of the OT devices. The agreement is to build a SOAR
platform that will provide the orchestration, automation and response. This is in addition to the vulnerability
management and penetration testing requirements. Both teams have agreed that all products need to be
cloud native and should include the use of a SIEM. The CIO wants to use Azure Sentinel as the SIEM but the
CISO want to use Google Chronicle. This SIEM will also be used to monitor the security of the data lake. The
CISO wants to use Qualys as the vulnerability management tool as it is currently being used in ACME.

 Outline the requirements for SOAR in the context of ACME’s operations.

 Explain the security controls that need to be implemented for the proposed SOAR solution.
 Evaluate the Azure Sentinel and Google Chronicle SIEM solutions and provide your recommendation.
Please explain the criteria you will be using for your analysis (you do not need to consider criteria
which are not directly impacting the security operations, e.g. costs).
 Explain the operational procedures the SOC team needs to create to deal with security threats.
 Please explain how you will integrate different data sources into the SIEM to create a single pane of
glass view that will provide security analysts the relevant information.
 Investigate if the Qualys vulnerability management tool is the best tool for ACME and if you feel this is
not the right tool, please provide a gap analysis and recommend the tool that should be used.
 Explain the pentesting approach and define the scope of the pentesting program.

write a 5 minute script for backrooms using the level data below. Make sure the story is from the view point of one person with a camcorder in the 1990s. try to make it interesting and creepy.

level 122 is the 123rd level of The Backrooms, and was discovered on 9/12/2020.
Description:

Level 122 has the appearance of a 1980s never-ending strip mall with neon lighting lining the roof and floor of the level. In this level supplies appear in large quantities and are found in the store outlets around the level, devices brought into this level can be used constantly without charge as it has been reported that the light rays from the neon strips have a charging effect on devices. Music speakers line the roof and oftentimes play music from the 1980s to around the end of 2006 which can keep you from going insane and has been proven to slightly heal victims of phycological attacks in the Backrooms. The level is oddly very clean compared to other backrooms levels, the reason behind this is unknown but it is thought that there is a group of explorers on Level 122 that maintain the level's cleanliness. The information is not yet confirmed. The lights lining the building determine the level's difficulty by changing colour at random intervals, the music played on the speakers during these stages also changes, below is a list of the colours that the lights have been documented to change to.
Color of Lighting Decode:

    Red=Critical State: In this state, entities appear in large numbers and have been documented to be overly aggressive toward wanderers, any methods of deterring or defeating entities have been proven useless during this state.
    Yellow=Hazardous: Entities are in their normal state but are wandering the level in larger numbers. Taking shelter in vendors or The Food Court as well as teaming up with other wanderers is recommended as a single wanderer is unlikely to take down a group of entities.
    Purple=Neutral state: This colour is around the majority of the time being documented as the longest-lasting emitted colour of the lights, the mall is in no worse or better state. Entities will still wander the level but they are less of a threat as the number of entities is decreased to a "tolerable" state.
    Pink=Entity Obedience: Pink-colored lights is a blessing to underequipped wanderers in Level 122, in this state entities of any aggressiveness are non-hostile towards wanderers. It is recommended to not approach entities even while these colours are around due to the short period these lights last.

These lights have also been documented to shut down at random times leading to blackout phases (See below)
Blackout Phases:

Every once in a while on Level 122, the neon strips will shut down leaving travellers of Level 122 in complete darkness at first, after some seconds, the only sources of lights will be the warm soft glow of the neon store signs, the skylights of the mall is dark as well but have small white dots depicting stars in the night sky. Smilers wander the level during blackout phases so it is recommended to stay at The Food Court or carry lots of almond water with you. Blackout phases average around 2 hours before the hum of generators can be heard and everything comes back online, with lights flickering back on and the music crackling back to sound.
Shopping Centers:

Vendors line the walls of Level 122 but most of them have a pull-down iron garage door hiding the interior of the store. Only a few stores have been found open and operating, the stores found open consisted of differently themed stores across the eras of the human race such as a 1990s music record vendor, a 1970s candy parlour, a 2008 tech store and so on. One report has found a store filled with objects from the Backrooms ranging from Memory Jars, the Ultimate Backrooms Cookbook, bottles of Almond Water, and various makeshift weapons such as a broken bottle attached to a leather wristband and boxing gloves which seem to be made of various materials from different Backrooms levels. Other vendors that have been found open are usually rotten and deceased where Wormlings are found feasting on whatever junk is left on the floor and Smilers usually lurk in the back of run-down stores.
Entities:

Level 122 houses varying types of entities most of which are aggressive towards wanders, the documented entities of this level include the following.

    Wormlings are found in rotten and run-down store outlets feasting on remains of what seem to be animals from reality.
    Smilers lurk in the far reaches of run-down stores and appear during blackout phases.
    Although being mostly docile, Skin Stealers are only found during the critical stage of Level 122.
    Inside of the Backrooms Vendor among the other objects inside the store, Gossip Beacons of all documented colours can be found on shelves but don’t seem to have any psychological effects on wanders while inside the store. Some Gossip Beacons have been found with the LED light inside of them turned off.
    Claimed by one wanderer of Level 122, the Memory Worm was seen wandering the level during the Hazardous state of Level 122 at a speed undocumented in its original discovery. This claim is disregarded as since then no sightings of the Memory Worm have been discussed.
    What once was a fully documented entity, The Server is an exclusive entity to Level 122. Only found in The Food Court, his entire body is nonexistent yet you can tell he is still present due to his clothes floating in place of his body, he wears an old-fashioned ice cream parlour outfit and has a very outgoing voice reminding you of those advertisements that used to run late into the night in reality. Although being a very docile and usually friendly entity, angering him can lead to getting kicked out of The Food Court and sometimes physical fights with the invisible man himself.

Outposts:
The Food Court

    The Food Court has no exact place in Level 122, it is relatively easy to find as clues are laying around the level such as discarded food plates and soda stains on the floor. These clues will eventually lead you to find The Food Court.
    The Food Court is one of the safest places in this level, it has been reported that The Server deters entities with a large pitchfork and special spices from his kitchen. It is unknown what these spices may contain to fend off large groups of entities.
    The Food Court houses the exclusive entity of Level 122, The Server. The Server is willing to provide you with food, Almond Water, Royal Rations, and sometimes other supplies like clothing that can temporarily protect against acidic burns and tools from his kitchen, such as a cleaver or steak knives.
    The Server often declines trades with wanderers of Level 122, he prefers cash or coins for items rather than trading an object for another one. The Server is NOT a fair man and oftentimes will market items or food at ridiculous prices or not pay you back after noticing defects or oddities in the item being bought, attempting to haggle with The Server to lower the prices has been reported to get you kicked out of the food court until further notice. One report of haggling with The Server led to a wanderer being struck by the ungloved hand of the entity, the victim was later reported dead and covered in a type of bacteria across his face and seeping onto his torso.

Entrances And Exits:
Entrances

    Tripping and falling in a four-way intersection on Level 109 will cause you to blackout and later wake up in Level 122
    Rarely some doors in Level 480 will lead to Level 122 but it is hard to differentiate an entrance to another store and an entrance to Level 122 as the doors look identical

Exits

    Jumping into a trash can by the food court can lead to a random restaurant in Level 11.
    Walking around the level for approximately 13 hours will put you in front of 2 bathroom entrances labelled with familiar symbols instead of the usual Male and Female signs, taking the one on the right with the sign of a paint splatter will lead to level Unnamed.
    Deciding to take the bathroom entrance on the left with the sign of a distorted train can lead to walking out of a bathroom in The Metro
    Finding a poster of an arcade and touching it will lead to Level 40
    Finding a store labelled “Artopoieío Desire” and entering it will lead to Level 67
    Some doors next to store outlets will be dirtier than the rest of the surrounding level, entering these will lead to Level 480 oftentimes putting you in the centre of an aisle in a random store with the door disappearing behind you.
    Reported by a few wanderers, during blackout phases, unlocking and opening the door next to the Backrooms Vendor has a chance of leading to the Blackout, this is not recommended as there is no escape out of the Blackout.
    Asking The Server for “The Usual” will lead him to the backside of his kitchen, once in there you will hear him whipping up some type of dish and smell the last thing you have consumed, after about 4 minutes you’ll blackout and the last level you were thinking of is where you will wake up at.
